jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Position Overview:**

The Florin Road Partnership (FRP) seeks a experienced Executive Director to lead the organization’s mission. The Executive Director will oversee the FRP's day\-to\-day operations and work closely with the Board of Directors to implement strategic initiatives to create a clean, safe, and thriving business district. As the Executive Director, you will be responsible for vendor management, stakeholder engagement, marketing, overseeing development projects, and financial management. You will also be the primary liaison with local government bodies and the public, ensuring the FRP's goals are met efficiently.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Operational Leadership:** 

* Oversee daily operations and the successful execution of FRP’s goals and objectives.
* Manage and cooperate with vendors for services such as cleaning, safety, and beautification efforts.
* Communicate with vendors on safety and maintenance issues .
* Maintain relationships with property and business owners to foster collaboration.

**Stakeholder Engagement and Communication:**

* Act as the primary point of contact between the FRP and local government, utilities, and law enforcement agencies.
* Lead public engagement initiatives and represent the FRP at community and stakeholder meetings.
* Promote the FRP’s mission and projects through effective communication strategies.
* Lead district marketing, outreach, and communications efforts to strengthen relationships with property owners, businesses, public agencies, and community partners while promoting district programs and initiatives.

**Financial and Administrative Oversight:** 

* Collaborate with the Board to set short\- and long\-term strategies for district development.
* Assist with executing development projects, including streetscape improvements and other infrastructure enhancements.
* Manage the FRP’s budget and monitor the financial health of the organization.
* Oversee grant applications, ensuring compliance with funding requirements.
* Maintain accurate records and databases for property and business owners.

**Qualifications:** 

* Bachelor’s degree or four years of management experience in a nonprofit or improvement district setting, and experience in community development, placemaking, and PBID operations are desirable.
* MS Office, Mailchimp, and Canva experience
* Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills.
* Proven experience in financial management
* Experience in collaborating and communicating with outside day\-to\-day vendor operations
* Ability to effectively engage with diverse stakeholders, including property owners, business leaders, community members, and public officials.

**To apply for this position, please send a resume, cover letter, and three references to this posting or send it to info@florinroad.com**

Pay: $85,000\.00 \- $98,500\.00 per year

Work Location: In person

jobs.description:
**Please use Google Chrome or Mozilla Firefox when accessing Candidate Home.**
==============================================================================

***By joining the American Red Cross you will touch millions of lives every year and experience the greatness of the human spirit at its best. Are you ready to be part of the world's largest humanitarian network?***
=======================================================================================================================================================================================================================

**Join us—Where your Career is a Force for Good!**
==================================================

**Job Description:**

Joining the American Red Cross is like nothing else – it’s as much something you feel as something you do. You become a vital part of the world’s largest humanitarian network. Joining a team of welcoming individuals who are exceptional yet unassuming. Diverse, yet uncompromising in unity. You grow your career within a movement that matters, where success is measured in people helped, communities made whole, and individuals equipped to never stop changing lives and situations for the better.


When you choose to be a force for good, you’ll have mentors who empower your growth along a purposeful career path. You align your life’s work with an ongoing mission that’s bigger than all of us. As you care for others, you’re cared for with competitive compensation and benefits. You join a community that respects who you are away from work as much as what you do while at work.


**Where Your Career is a Force for Good!**

The American Red Cross is seeking a **Chief Development Officer** to support the California Gold Country Region. This region supports 31 counties in Northern California. This is a hybrid position. Incumbents will spend several days a week in the office and the remainder of their time in the field or in a home office.


**WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW:**

In this role, you will lead a team of professional fundraisers to strengthen and grow the region’s donor base to meet its assigned financial target of more than $10M. The Chief Development Officer will provide leadership to the staff and volunteers in planning, managing, and implementing fundraising development strategies for the assigned segment(s) of giving. They will also assist in cultivating donors and sponsors to meet monthly, quarterly, and annual revenue targets while maintaining their own portfolios. The Director is responsible for managing an assigned team, including hiring, training, coaching, evaluating, and managing performance. This role is not eligible for relocation assistance.


**WHERE YOUR CAREER IS A FORCE GOOD:**

* Manage a portfolio of high\-level donors and prospects within the assigned segment to achieve the annual revenue target, including both renewable gifts and new incremental revenue.
* Develop and implement a plan for revenue growth from donors at the $5,000\+ level with an emphasis on maximizing revenue for the American Red Cross. The individual segment will collaborate with the Gift Planning team to strategically grow planned giving revenue across major donor\-managed accounts for individuals and families. The corporate and foundation segments’ strategic growth plan could include (but are not limited to) engagement and growth of giving societies such as the Tiffany Circle, serving as a staff liaison to the Board's Philanthropy Committee and developing and implementing strategies for board giving and influence for the region, growing cause marketing and overall corporate revenue by assisting corporations in achieving their goals to strengthen their brand, marketing, and employee engagement, leading the region's special events program, or leading a strategy to identify, cultivate, and engage foundations to grow their support for the American Red Cross.
* Cultivate relationships and serve as primary staff liaison for major donors and conduct regular joint visits with relationship managers and donors when those relationships are managed by members of your team.
* Collaborate with national headquarters development team members to maximize fundraising opportunities and implement policies and procedures to ensure the success of the region's fundraising efforts.
* Oversee the implementation of organizational campaigns in the region.
* Serve as a senior leader in the Region and proactively drive the implementation of strategic decisions for the organization. Ensure that the Region meets all reporting requirements related to fundraising, including ensuring up\-to\-date donor records in Salesforce.
* Provide strong leadership, develop, and set individual and team performance goals and manage/drive change.
* Hire, manage, train, and evaluate staff and volunteers to ensure fundraising results and that all team members meet or exceed national/regional fundraising metrics.
* Lead and execute disaster relief fundraising efforts as appropriate.

***WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED:***

**Education:** Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience required.


**Experience:** Minimum 7 years of sales/fundraising experience or equivalent combination of education and related experience required. Experience with increasing philanthropic revenue from diverse constituencies, developing and implementing broad\-based fundraising programs, and knowledge of “best practices” in development.


**Management Experience:** 5 years of management experience.
  

**Skills \& Abilities:** Ability to relate well and work effectively with multiple constituencies and audiences. Excellent interpersonal, verbal and written skills. Knowledge of office systems: MS Office preferred and fundraising database systems (e.g. Raiser’s Edge, Salesforce.com). Ability to work on a team within a collaborative environment and ensure the highest customer service orientation.

jobs.title: Director of Operations (with COO development track)
jobs.description:
Curtis Legal Group is seeking a strategic, driven, and enthusiastic, Director of

Operations to join our personal injury practice and serve as an operational leader

supporting the firm’s C\-Suite, attorneys, and legal teams. This role is designed for

an operations leader who thrives in translating firm strategy into scalable systems,

clear accountability, and consistent execution across legal teams.

Curtis Legal Group values collaboration, integrity, clarity, and high standards. The

Director of Operations plays a critical role in strengthening how legal work is

supported, supervised, and delivered without practicing law or directing legal

judgment.

**Position Overview**

The Director of Operations is a position tracking to the position of Chief Operations

Officer (COO). This person is responsible for assisting stakeholders with firm\-wide

oversight of legal support operations, case workflow execution, performance

management, and cross\-functional alignment between attorneys, operations,

finance, intake, and administration. This role focuses on optimizing legal delivery

systems, improving consistency across offices and teams, and supporting attorney

effectiveness and capacity.

**Reporting Relationship**

Reports directly to the C\-Suite team of the Firm.

**Authority \& Scope**

* Assists operations across all practice locations
* Establishes firm\-wide legal operational standards, workflows, and KPIs
* Deploys artificial intelligence solutions
* Partners with legal leadership on execution while respecting attorney autonomy and ethical boundaries
* Reviews and provides analysis of firm operations, including budgets in order to develop efficiencies and to scale profitability
* Creates innovative solutions for firm operations and reviews data analytics, including use of AI support
* Serves as the C\-Suite representative of the management team and oversees office work culture in the Sacramento office
* Assists management team with firm marketing strategies and advertising
* mediums

**Core Responsibilities**

**Operations Leadership**

* Assists in oversight of the execution and consistency of legal workflows from intake handoff through case resolution
* Monitors KPIs including but not limited to firm’s AI usage, client service, attorney revenue, case progression, attorney and legal team caseload capacity, case resolution time and obstacles
* Collaborates with Managing Attorneys to align operational systems with legal strategy and client outcomes
* Develops and maintains standard operating procedures (SOPs) supporting legal teams
* Assists HR to recruit and develop existing and future team members
* People Leadership \& Accountability
* Assists with development of legal operation supervisors, managers, case managers, and support leaders
* Demonstrates and advocates firm values in the role as a leader of team members
* Supports attorney performance through clarity of roles, expectations, and tools—not legal direction
* Drives, manages, and demonstrates a growth mindset in firm performance, training, mentorship, and professional development
* Cross\-Functional \& Strategic Support
* Acts as the primary operational liaison between legal teams and firm leadership

* Creates and develops operational efficiencies and strategy for firm profitability

**Qualifications**

* Master's degree in business administration (MBA) /management or demonstrated operational leadership experience
* Exhibits and/or demonstrates skills required for success in leading

* Experience in professional services or legal services
* Experience with artificial intelligence (AI) programs and systems

jobs.title: Program Director - Lake County
jobs.description:
**About Redwood Community Services**
------------------------------------


Redwood Community Services (RCS) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening individuals, families, and communities through behavioral health, child welfare, youth development, housing, and support services. Our work changes lives every day, and our employees make that possible.


We are seeking an experienced, strategic, and collaborative Director of Lake County Programs to provide leadership and oversight for a diverse portfolio of behavioral health, child welfare, youth development, and community\-based services throughout Lake County. The ideal candidate is a mission\-driven leader with strong clinical knowledge, operational expertise, and a passion for supporting children, youth, families, and communities. This role requires the ability to balance program quality, fiscal stewardship, staff development, community partnerships, and innovative service delivery while ensuring excellence in care and outcomes.

**What You'll Do**


Key responsibilities include:


* Provide overall leadership, direction, and oversight for Lake County programs, ensuring high\-quality services across behavioral health, rehabilitation, substance use disorder treatment, foster care, wraparound, residential, and school\-based programs.
* Oversee clinical and program operations while promoting effective coordination of services for children, youth, families, and individuals with complex behavioral health and social service needs.
* Monitor program performance, revenue, and expenditures to ensure fiscal responsibility, sustainability, and alignment with organizational goals.
* Participate in strategic planning, program development, and implementation of new services that respond to emerging community needs and funding opportunities.
* Collaborate with clinical leadership to support treatment planning, program reviews, quality improvement activities, and resolution of reportable incidents and service concerns.
* Provide leadership in budget planning, review financial performance, and authorize expenditures consistent with approved budgets and organizational priorities.
* Recruit, interview, hire, supervise, mentor, and develop program managers, supervisors, clinicians, rehabilitation specialists, and support staff.
* Foster a positive, collaborative culture through staff development, training, coaching, performance management, and succession planning efforts.
* Work closely with leadership teams across RCS to ensure seamless coordination among programs and alignment with the agency's mission, values, and strategic objectives.
* Establish and maintain productive partnerships with county agencies, schools, child welfare organizations, behavioral health providers, regional centers, community partners, and other stakeholders.
* Represent RCS within community collaboratives, public meetings, and professional organizations to strengthen partnerships and advance services for vulnerable populations.
* Ensure compliance with licensing requirements, contractual obligations, organizational policies, and applicable regulatory standards while promoting continuous quality improvement.
* Prepare and review program reports, operational data, and outcome measures to support informed decision\-making and effective communication with executive leadership and the Board of Directors.
* Respond to complex operational issues, critical incidents, and crisis situations while maintaining a focus on safety, service quality, and positive outcomes.
* Support community engagement, public relations, fundraising, special projects, and organizational initiatives as assigned.

**What We're Looking For**


The successful candidate will bring:


* A master's degree in Behavioral Science, Social Work, Psychology, Counseling, or a related field.
* California BBS licensure (or eligibility consistent with program requirements).
* Significant leadership and supervisory experience in behavioral health, child welfare, youth services, or related community\-based programs.
* Experience managing complex programs, budgets, contracts, and multidisciplinary teams.
* Strong clinical judgment, strategic thinking, and problem\-solving skills.
* Excellent communication, relationship\-building, and community partnership abilities.
* A commitment to trauma\-informed, culturally responsive, family\-centered, and strengths\-based services.

**Why Join RCS?**


At RCS, you'll have the opportunity to lead meaningful programs that improve the lives of children, youth, and families while working alongside dedicated professionals who share a commitment to service, innovation, and community impact. You'll play a key role in shaping programs, developing staff, strengthening partnerships, and helping advance our mission of building healthier and stronger communities throughout Lake County.

**We offer:**

* Meaningful work with a respected nonprofit organization
* Collaborative and supportive team environment
* Opportunities for professional development
* Stable, mission\-focused employment
* Comprehensive benefits package (for eligible employees)
* The opportunity to make a real impact every day

**Apply Today****If you're passionate about accuracy, enjoy working behind the scenes to support employees, and want to be part of an organization that changes lives, we'd love to hear from you.**
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

jobs.title: President & CEO
jobs.description:
**To Apply:** Submit your Resume, Cover Letter and 3 Professional References electronically to **CEOJOB@sgvpartnership.org** by **July 19, 2026 for priority review**. We will continue to accept applications after this date, however they will be reviewed after the *priority* submissions.

This position will remain open until filled.

***\-\-Applications will ONLY be accepted via email, applications submitted through Indeed will NOT be reviewed\-\-***

**About the Opportunity**

The San Gabriel Valley Economic Partnership (SGVEP) is seeking an experienced, visionary, and collaborative leader to serve as its next President \& Chief Executive Officer. For more than 30 years, the Partnership has served as the leading voice for business and economic development in the San Gabriel Valley, representing 31 cities and nearly two million residents throughout eastern Los Angeles County.

**Position Overview**

Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the President \& CEO serves as the chief executive officer of the organization and is responsible for providing strategic leadership, organizational management, fiscal oversight, membership growth, stakeholder engagement, advocacy, and program development.

The successful candidate will be a dynamic relationship\-builder who can effectively work with elected officials, business leaders, educational institutions, workforce partners, and community stakeholders while positioning the Partnership as the premier regional economic development organization in the San Gabriel Valley.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Strategic Leadership**

* Lead the implementation and execution of the Partnership's Strategic Plan.
* Collaborate with the Board of Directors to establish organizational priorities, goals, and performance measures.
* Identify emerging economic, workforce, and public policy opportunities affecting the San Gabriel Valley.
* Position the Partnership as a recognized regional leader on issues impacting business competitiveness and economic growth.

**Organizational Management**

* Oversee the day\-to\-day operations of the organization and provide leadership to staff and consultants.
* Develop annual work plans, budgets, and organizational objectives.
* Ensure effective governance support for the Board of Directors and its committees.
* Foster a high\-performing, mission\-driven organizational culture.

**Revenue Development \& Membership Growth**

* Lead membership recruitment, retention, and engagement efforts.
* Develop and implement strategies to diversify and grow organizational revenue.
* Secure sponsorships, grants, partnerships, and other funding opportunities.
* Cultivate relationships with corporate, public\-sector, philanthropic, and community partners.

**Regional Advocacy \& External Relations**

* Serve as the chief spokesperson for the organization.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with elected officials, business executives, city managers, educational leaders, and community stakeholders.
* Represent the Partnership at regional, state, and national meetings and events.
* Advance public policy initiatives that support economic development, workforce development, infrastructure investment, housing, and business growth.

**Program \& Economic Development Leadership**

* Guide the development and implementation of programs and initiatives that support the Partnership's mission.
* Promote the San Gabriel Valley as a destination for investment, business attraction, and job creation.
* Strengthen collaboration among cities, educational institutions, employers, and workforce development partners.
* Oversee signature events, regional convenings, and economic development initiatives.

**Desired Qualifications**

The ideal candidate will possess:

* Demonstrated executive leadership experience in economic development, business organizations, chambers of commerce, public administration, nonprofit management, workforce development, or a related field.
* Proven success in organizational leadership, strategic planning, and revenue generation.
* Strong financial management and budgeting experience.
* Exceptional communication, public speaking, and relationship\-building skills.
* Experience working with boards of directors, elected officials, and diverse stakeholder groups.
* A track record of developing partnerships and leading collaborative initiatives.
* Knowledge of economic development, workforce development, public policy, and regional business issues.

**Education Requirements** 

A **bachelor's degree** from an accredited college or university is **required**. A master's degree in public administration, business administration, economic development, or a related field is preferred.

**Compensation \& Benefits**

The annual salary range for this position is **$135,000 to $170,000**, depending on qualifications and experience.

In addition, the Board of Directors may consider performance\-based compensation enhancements tied to the achievement of mutually agreed\-upon organizational revenue growth and fundraising targets. Any such incentives would be negotiated between the President \& CEO and the Executive Committee of the Board of Directors.

**The Partnership offers a competitive benefits package, including:**

* Employer\-paid medical insurance
* 401(k) retirement plan with a 3% employer match
* Four\-and\-one\-half\-day work week (half\-day Fridays)
* Three weeks paid vacation
* 80 hours of sick leave
* 48 hours of personal leave
* Ten paid holidays annually

**Application Process**

Interested candidates should submit:

* Cover Letter
* Resume
* Three Professional References\*

*\*References will only be contacted as part of a background check for a candidate that receives and accepts a conditional job offer.* 

Applications and questions should be submitted electronically to: **CEOJOB@sgvpartnership.org**  
**\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\

jobs.title: Executive Director
jobs.description:
**Executive Director**

**Position Description**

**Position Summary**

The Executive Director (ED) will provide leadership and direction to fulfill First Tee North Coast’s mission, vision and goals. Inspired by a genuine passion for empowering and helping youth succeed in life, ED will work diligently to promote and expand the efforts of the organization.

Reporting to and partnering with the FTNC Board of Directors, the ED will have overall strategic and operational responsibility for FTNC staff, programs, expansion and execution of the strategic plan.

The ED will be a highly visible member of the community, representing FTNC throughout the service area, including Marin, Sonoma, and Napa counties. The ED will embody FTNC’s strong commitment to its youth and will promote the organization throughout the community, accelerating its impact, resource development and brand recognition. The ED will be instrumental in helping FTNC achieve its full potential.

*Key Criteria for success of the role include*:

· Achieving strong fundraising success through a robust development and marketing communications program, including expanded engagement with individuals, corporations and foundations;

· Embodying a clear, inspiring, entrepreneurial vision and guiding the team to translate those strategies into effectively executed plans;

· Partnering effectively with the Board of Directors and staff to build on the success of the program to continually innovate and improve outcomes for youth;

· Ensuring program effectiveness through evaluation of quantitative and qualitative program analytics to articulate a comprehensive impact story;

· Expand and scale FTNC’s programming in additional high priority, at risk neighborhoods;

· Ability to collaborate effectively with the FTNC Board of Directors to develop strong board governance and fundraising practices.

**Responsibilities**

*Strategy:* ED will lead, partnering with the board, to develop strategies and goals to align and coordinate efforts to grow agency operations, programs, and resources. ED will create an annual development plan supporting the chapter strategic plan. ED will have fiscal responsibility of the organization along with responsibility to budget /forecast to align with the strategic plan.

*Fundraising:* ED, along with the Board and with the direction of the Fundraising Committee, identify, cultivate, solicit, and steward contributed revenues to support the organization while also activating key partners and volunteers to provide meaningful assistance. ED will establish revenue goals tied to strategic priorities and share quantitative progress assessments. Creative, donor\-centric stewardship is to be complemented by relationship and mission\-based acquisition of new donors of all levels, including major donors with the capacity to retain major gifts.

*Governance:* The ED and the board Governance Committee will work together, collaboratively, to determine how the organization can best achieve its mission. The ED will support each committee in their roles and responsibilities and work to facilitate a strong recruitment and development process.

*Marketing:* The ED will leverage national collateral with recently created brand insight work for FTNC to promote local approach to youth development through the game of golf. ED will be the local storyteller\-in\-residence and communicate the donor\-centric stories of impact and opportunities to empower kids by putting them on course for life.

*Management \& Compliance:* ED will provide energetic leadership, support, and recruitment assistance to the FTNC Board of Directors; delegate and oversee staff, and plan and oversee growth of office infrastructure. ED will ensure the FTNC chapter maintains compliance with the First Tee national organization.

**Qualifications**

· Nonprofit executive experience and philanthropic community engagement with a demonstrated history of success in coordinating and implementing fundraising strategies, grant/proposal writing, fundraising events, major gifts, campaigns, donor relationship building, and planned giving, preferably in the youth development field, and within the CA north bay communities including Marin, Sonoma, and Napa counties.

· Executive experience with nonprofit financials, budgeting, and fiscal management. Management of staff, contractors, and volunteers, including developing and collaborating with a nonprofit governing board, strategic partner alliances, and with the procurement of resources for direct\-service youth development programs.

· Passionate youth development ambassador with experience promoting access to and involvement in programs for young participants and their families, especially within underrepresented and underserved populations, and with volunteers, donors, and donor prospects and allied community partners.

· General knowledge of sports and its positive impact for youth development programming, particular with the game of golf, preferred.

· Confident strategic thinker, driven by conviction and knowledge, reliant on data analytics and context to inform decision\-making, with the fortitude to guide others in these practices.

· Excellent transparent and inclusive communicator who follows through and follows up. Ability to share thoughts and observations, and who consistently treats others equitably and with respect.

· Well\-organized and energetic; a lead\-by\-example doer, committed to personal excellence in leadership and execution who will uphold ethical standards and values of the nonprofit profession and FTNC.

· Knowledgeable professional, and also an avid and enthusiastic learner willing to ask questions and pursue professional development for self as well as staff and volunteers, fostering an active culture of philanthropic service and teamwork.

**To Apply**

Please send resume’ and cover letter to Pepper Peete.

Salary Range $110,000 \- $130,000

jobs.title: Chief Operating Officer
jobs.description:
**We're Searching for Our Next Chief Operating Officer**

Not simply to manage a law firm. **But to help continue building one of the most innovative family law firms in the country.**

Laughlin Legal, PC represents founders, venture capital professionals, technology executives, entrepreneurs, physicians, professional athletes, and other sophisticated clients throughout Silicon Valley and California. Our matters frequently involve complex financial issues, executive compensation, founder equity, private equity, closely held businesses, international issues, and high\-conflict custody litigation.

Over the past thirteen years, we have built one of California's fastest\-growing family law firms. Our gross revenue has increased by approximately 25% to 50% year over year for thirteen consecutive years.

Why? Because we are relentless in our pursuit of excellence. We strive to provide the highest level of legal representation and client service in family law in California. We invest in exceptional people, embrace innovation, continually refine our systems, and refuse to become complacent. We believe that when you build an extraordinary team and deliver an extraordinary client experience, sustained growth naturally follows.

Just as importantly, we believe extraordinary results should never come at the expense of extraordinary people.

We have intentionally built a collaborative, collegial culture where people genuinely enjoy working together, support one another, and are committed to each other's success. We value excellence, but we also value balance. Unlike many firms, our attorneys are expected to bill only **1,400 hours per year**, allowing them to spend meaningful time with their families, pursue personal interests, and build fulfilling lives outside the office.

We reject the notion that burnout is the price of success. Our experience has proven otherwise. By investing in exceptional people, creating outstanding systems, embracing innovation, and fostering a culture people are proud to be part of, we have achieved remarkable growth while maintaining a workplace where talented professionals choose to build long\-term careers.

**We're looking for an exceptional Chief Operating Officer who is energized by the opportunity to continue building one of the most innovative family law firms in the country.**

This is a true executive leadership position. You will serve as my strategic partner in shaping the future of the firm while leading our operations, people, technology, and long\-term growth. You will have a meaningful voice in the future direction of the firm and the opportunity to help build an organization that sets a new standard for family law.

Key Responsibilities

* Executive leadership and strategic planning
* Recruiting, retaining, and developing exceptional attorneys and professional staff
* Associate coaching, mentoring, leadership development, and performance management
* Elevating every aspect of the client experience
* Optimizing Clio and leveraging its full capabilities across the firm
* Designing scalable systems, workflows, and operational excellence
* Leading the implementation of artificial intelligence and automation throughout the firm
* Coordinating marketing and business development initiatives
* Financial management, budgeting, billing, collections, profitability, and key performance metrics
* Developing and implementing strategic growth and expansion initiatives
* Helping design the firm's future leadership and partnership structure

The Ideal Candidate Will Have

* Significant executive leadership experience within a successful law firm
* Demonstrated success building high\-performing teams and scalable operations
* Extensive experience with Clio (Clio Certified Consultant or Clio Partner experience is strongly preferred)
* Exceptional financial, operational, and business acumen
* Experience implementing technology and AI solutions to improve efficiency and client service
* Outstanding emotional intelligence, leadership, judgment, and communication skills
* An entrepreneurial mindset with an unwavering commitment to excellence
* A passion for building organizations, developing people, and creating a culture where others thrive

We are building more than a successful law firm.

We are building a place where exceptional professionals can do the best work of their careers without sacrificing the people and experiences that matter most. We believe a law firm can be both extraordinarily successful and deeply human. We believe innovation, kindness, accountability, and excellence can coexist. After thirteen years of sustained growth, we have demonstrated that they do.

If our next chapter excites you, we'd love to meet you.

Compensation

* **Base salary:** $280,000 \- $300,000, depending on experience
* Performance bonus
* Comprehensive benefits
* 3 weeks vacation
* 11 paid holidays
* Sick leave

**Hybrid position. You must live in the SF Bay Area.**

Please send your résumé and a cover letter explaining why this opportunity speaks to you to **careers@laughlinlegal.com**.

**Great law firms are not built by great lawyers alone. They are built by visionary leaders who know how to attract exceptional people, create extraordinary systems, embrace innovation, and build organizations that endure.**

If you're excited by the opportunity to help build one of the premier family law firms in the nation, we'd love to hear from you!

Pay: $280,000\.00 \- $300,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Foster City, CA 94404

jobs.title: Executive Director--Help Build the Future of Memory Care
jobs.description:
**Now Hiring: Executive Director – Aroha Memory Care (Santa Rosa, CA)**  
**Location:** Santa Rosa, CA (Oakmont Village)  
**Status:** Full\-Time, Exempt  
**Organization:** Aroha Memory Care by MD Senior Wellness  
**Reports To:** Founder \& Owner, Raj Kalra, MD

**Some executive directors manage communities. Others help transform an industry.**

At Aroha Memory Care, we believe older adults living with dementia deserve more than traditional memory care. They deserve an environment that promotes wellness, purpose, dignity, and joy. We are seeking an *exceptional* Executive Director to lead one of the most innovative physician\-founded memory care communities in California while helping redefine what dementia care can become. This is more than an Executive Director position. It is an opportunity to help build a new model of senior living that integrates evidence\-based medicine, lifestyle medicine, hospitality, technology, and person\-centered dementia care.

*If you are passionate about leadership, innovation, and making a meaningful difference in the lives of older adults and their families, we would love to meet you.*

**About Aroha Memory Care** Aroha Memory Care is a newly opened, wellness\-focused, 22\-suite luxury memory care community located in the beautiful Oakmont Village of Sonoma County. Our mission is simple: To improve the wellness, dignity, and quality of life of older adults living with dementia while supporting the families who love them. Unlike traditional memory care communities, Aroha combines:

* Evidence\-based dementia care
* Physician\-lead Lifestyle Medicine
* Personalized wellness programming
* Innovative technology
* Hospitality\-inspired service
* Beautiful purpose\-built design

Every decision we make is guided by one question: "Will this improve the life of our residents?"

**Work Alongside the Founder** As Executive Director, you will work directly with Raj Kalra, MD, Founder of Aroha Memory Care and MD Senior Wellness.

After more than 16 years in physician leadership, Dr. Kalra founded Aroha with a vision of transforming senior living through evidence\-based care, innovation, wellness, and compassionate leadership. Rather than simply overseeing daily operations, you will collaborate closely with Dr. Kalra to continue developing Aroha's unique care model, refine operational systems, introduce new technologies, strengthen community partnerships, and create an organization that serves as a model for the future of dementia care.

**Growth Opportunity** Aroha Memory Care is the first community developed by MD Senior Wellness, an organization dedicated to transforming how we care for older adults. Our long\-term vision extends beyond operating a single exceptional memory care community. jobs.title: EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, RACIAL EQUITY
jobs.description:
The County of Los Angeles Chief Executive Office is recruiting for an Executive Director, Racial Equity (UC). The Executive Director, Racial Equity will lead the Los Angeles County's enterprise racial equity strategy by providing executive leadership for the County’s Anti\-Racism, Diversity, and Inclusion (ARDI) initiative and serving as the County’s principal advisor on organizational transformation, equity strategy, implementation, and systems change. Reporting within the Chief Executive Office, the Executive Director will provide vision, strategic direction, and operational leadership for countywide efforts to identify, address, and eliminate structural and systemic racism and advance equity and inclusion in the areas where the County has control or influence, including service delivery, policy development, budgeting, contracting and employment.  

  

The Executive Director will work across County departments and with community\-based organizations, residents, philanthropy, academic partners, local jurisdictions, and other stakeholders to advance an anti\-racist policy agenda, promote equity and inclusion, build institutional capacity, strengthen accountability, and improve outcomes for all Los Angeles County residents. The position will lead a multidisciplinary team and ensure that ARDI's work remains data\-informed, community\-centered, and aligned with the priorities of the Board of Supervisors.

jobs.title: Executive Director, Identity Lifecycle Management
jobs.description:
**JOB DESCRIPTION**  

  

  

Help shape the future of identity while protecting customers who trust us with sensitive information. In this role, you will make it simple and safe for customers to keep their identity data current and help catch and fix issues before they cause harm.


As an **executive director identity lifecycle** in **Trust and security**, you lead identity lifecycle management for customer demographic and identity updates, including phone, email, address, and legal name changes. You partner across channels, lines of business, and technology teams to deliver seamless customer experiences while putting strong protections in place during higher\-risk updates. You also own the strategy and roadmap for remediating potentially inaccurate or missing identity data and drive measurable improvements in data quality and account takeover prevention.


Job responsibilities

* Set the vision, strategy, and execution plan for how customers update identity and demographic data across channels.
* Partner with channels and lines of business to design and deliver simple, best\-in\-class update experiences.
* Build protections for high\-risk update moments, including possession and verification checks to help prevent account takeover.
* Create and deliver a roadmap to remediate potentially inaccurate or missing identity data, including proactive customer outreach and validation.
* Balance strong security with a smooth customer experience, reducing unnecessary friction for legitimate customers.
* Lead, mentor, and develop a team of product managers, building a culture of innovation, accountability, and excellence.
* Collaborate with technology, design, fraud, modeling, and operations teams to deliver scalable and reliable solutions.
* Define and track key metrics for customer experience, data quality, remediation progress, and account takeover outcomes.
* Establish and champion data governance standards, policies, and controls for identity lifecycle processes.
* Ensure solutions meet applicable privacy, policy, and audit requirements and maintain a strong control environment.
* Represent the team in senior leadership forums, clearly communicating strategy, risks, and results while keeping the roadmap forward\-looking.


Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills

* Proven experience as a product leader in identity, fraud, data management, customer experience, or related domains.
* Strong understanding of identity verification, possession checks, and account takeover prevention.
* Experience designing customer\-facing experiences across multiple channels and partnering across lines of business.
* Strong leadership skills, including managing and developing product teams and leading large cross\-functional programs.
* Demonstrated ability to build and execute roadmaps that deliver measurable customer, business, and risk outcomes.
* Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills, including simplifying complex topics for diverse audiences.
* Strong technical fluency across modern data platforms, cloud\-based architectures, and data pipelines.
* Solid understanding of data governance, privacy regulations, and compliance obligations related to identity data.
* Strong analytical, data\-driven approach to prioritization and decision\-making.
* Bachelor's degree in computer science, information systems, engineering, a related discipline, or equivalent professional experience.


Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills

* Demonstrated success building and launching complex technology or customer\-facing products in regulated industries.
* Advanced degree in a technical, analytical, or business field.
* Familiarity with identity and access management standards, protocols, and frameworks.
* Experience applying artificial intelligence or machine learning to identity, fraud, or risk use cases.
* Experience running large\-scale data remediation or customer outreach programs.
* Hands\-on experience with agile product development at enterprise scale.


Applicants must be authorized to work for any employer in the US. We are not able to provide immigration sponsorship or take over sponsorship of an employment visa at this time. Final job grade level and corporate title will be determined at time of offer and may differ from this posting.

Chase is a leading financial services firm, helping nearly half of America's households and small businesses achieve their financial goals through a broad range of financial products. Our mission is to create engaged, lifelong relationships and put our customers at the heart of everything we do. We also help small businesses, nonprofits and cities grow, delivering solutions to solve all their financial needs.


We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience, skill set and location. Those in eligible roles may receive commission\-based pay and/or discretionary incentive compensation, paid in the form of cash and/or forfeitable equity, awarded in recognition of individual achievements and contributions. We also offer a range of benefits and programs to meet employee needs, based on eligibility. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage, on\-site health and wellness centers, a retirement savings plan, backup childcare, tuition reimbursement, mental health support, financial coaching and more. Additional details about total compensation and benefits will be provided during the hiring process.

jobs.title: Associate Executive Director, NYC
jobs.description:
***If working for an organization that supports the land and people of Israel excites you, then join us and you needn't wait another minute to change the world!***

 **Position Overview:**


Jewish National Fund\-USA is expanding its Tristate fundraising team and adding several new positions to support our growing donor community and organizational impact.

  

We are seeking an experienced Associate Executive Director to oversee fundraising and community engagement in Manhattan, the Bronx, Brooklyn, Queens.This role focuses on building and sustaining strong donor relationships, partnering closely with lay leadership, and expanding regional support through strategic cultivation, events, and mission\-based engagement.

  



Reports to: Executive Director, Tristate


Location: New York, NY (on\-site, 5 days)

 **Responsibilities:**


Regional Leadership \& Strategy

* Develop and execute a regional fundraising and engagement strategy aligned with national priorities
* Set and manage revenue goals, performance metrics, and accountability standards
* Serve as a senior representative of JNF\-USA within the regional Jewish community


Fundraising \& Donor Engagement

* Manage and grow a portfolio of major donors and prospects through strategic cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship
* Identify and develop new donor relationships to expand the regional pipeline
* Partner with lay leadership and board members to leverage networks and advance fundraising outcomes
* Oversee cultivation initiatives, donor briefings, events, missions, and stewardship activities


Team Leadership \& Collaboration

* Lead, mentor, and develop a high\-performing regional team
* Support hiring, onboarding, coaching, and performance management
* Collaborate with national development, marketing, finance, and program teams


Community Engagement \& Representation

* Build and maintain strong relationships with communal partners and stakeholders
* Recruit and engage participants for missions, conferences, and signature initiatives
* Host visiting Israeli partners and affiliates with appropriate donor alignment and follow\-up


Operations, Data \& Reporting

* Ensure accurate donor activity tracking and reporting in Salesforce
* Use data and analytics to support forecasting, strategy, and performance management
* Oversee regional budgets and responsible resource management

 **Qualifications:**

* Deep passion for Israel and JNF\-USA's mission
* 10\+ years of progressive fundraising, development, or related leadership experience, including major gifts
* Proven ability to develop strategy and drive revenue growth
* Strong executive\-level written and verbal communication skills
* Demonstrated experience managing and developing teams
* Strategic, organized, and comfortable operating with autonomy
* Salesforce or CRM preferred
* Familiarity with the regional Jewish community and nonprofit landscape strongly preferred

 **Travel:**

* Regular regional travel within the assigned territory
* Occasional domestic and international travel, including missions to Israel and national conferences

 **Perks:**

* Days off for all work\-prohibitive Jewish holidays, in addition to the secular US holidays
* Generous paid time off
* 3pm close\-of\-business on Fridays
* Top\-notch healthcare (including dental and vision)
* Parental Leave policy
* Employer\-paid life insurance
* Employer\-paid short and long\-term disability
* 403(b) with 10\.5% employer contribution
* Opportunities to travel to Israel
* Occasional domestic and international travel, including missions to Israel and national conferences

 **Compensation:**


$175,000–$210,000

 **Pay Type:**


Salaried

  

Salaries will vary based on factors such as location, skills, experience, education, and qualifications for the role.

 **WHAT WE DO**


For 125 years, Jewish National Fund\-USA has helped strengthen the land and people of Israel by investing in the communities, infrastructure, and leadership that shape the country's future. Our work is focused on making Israel's North and South places where families can build full, vibrant lives with access to medical care, emergency response, water solutions, parks, playgrounds, jobs, and economic opportunity. Working with our donors and partners, we support underpopulated communities, help attract new residents, improve quality of life, and create the conditions for long\-term growth. Our work reaches beyond projects on the ground. Through education, travel, and leadership opportunities, we connect Americans of all ages to Israel in meaningful and lasting ways. That vision continues with the World Zionist Village in Be'er Sheva, a global center that will bring people together to live, learn, lead, and strengthen the next generation of Zionist leadership. From urgent relief to long\-term transformation, Jewish National Fund\-USA is helping build a stronger future for Israel and creating lasting legacies for generations to come. Learn more at jnf.org.

 **SINCE 1901, JNF HAS:**

* Planted more than 250 million trees
* Built over 250 reservoirs and dams
* Developed over 250,000 acres of land
* Created more than 2,000 parks
* Provided the infrastructure for over 1,000 communities
* Connected thousands of children and young adults to Israel and their heritage


Jewish National Fund prides itself on honoring the pioneers of the past and celebrating their spirit by continuing the pioneering tradition in the areas of technology, environmental progress, community development, water renewal and building the land of Israel for many years to come.


Jewish National Fund affirms the bonds of the Jewish family with their strength of unity, the understanding that each member can make a difference and the collective power to build prosperity, develop innovative solutions and promote the greater good.

 **OUR CREDENTIALS \& PARTNERSHIPS**


A registered 501(c)(3\) organization, Jewish National Fund is recognized as a 4\-star organization by Charity Nav

jobs.title: Managing Director, Discovery and Insight
jobs.description:
**Company Description** **ABOUT TED**


TED is on a mission to discover and spread ideas that spark imagination, embrace possibility, and catalyze impact. Our organization is devoted to curiosity, reason, wonder, and the pursuit of knowledge — without an agenda. We welcome people from every discipline and culture who seek a deeper understanding of the world and connection with others, and we invite everyone to engage with ideas and activate them in your community.

**ABOUT THE AUDACIOUS PROJECT**


The Audacious Project is a collaborative funding platform that invites visionary changemakers to imagine their boldest ideas to solve the world’s most urgent challenges.


Housed at TED, the nonprofit dedicated to the belief that ideas change everything, Audacious works to surface ideas that are breathtakingly ambitious yet achievable and brings together a community of philanthropists to unlock the funding needed to make them real. Each year, Audacious selects a cohort of extraordinary leaders from around the world — visionaries with proximity to the problems they're solving, and bold plans to shift systems, lift up communities, and contribute to a healthier planet.


Since its launch in 2018, Audacious has helped to catalyze more than $8B for over 70 projects across a range of issue areas. Rooted in TED's belief that ideas can come from anywhere, Audacious focuses on the strength of the idea and the team behind it, not a fixed issue area. Funded projects span science, climate, technology, health, social safety net and justice.

 **Job Description** **Role Description**


The Managing Director, Discovery and Insight leads Audacious's global search for ambitious ideas and changemakers — finding, testing, and building the case for the ideas and leaders most worth backing each funding cycle. This role owns end\-to\-end curation, application, and diligence activities, ultimately leading to recommendations for the cohort of finalists selected annually.


The Managing Director's work is centered on: a strategy informed by continuous learning about systems\-level impact and Audacious' unique role within it; deep collaboration with applicants, donors, and partners nurtured over time; and leadership of a strong team to sustain the work.


Each year, the Discovery and Insight team conducts a global search to identify nonprofit ideas that are:

* Innovative and offer a unique approach to a significant problem with the potential to shift the status quo or the system they operate within
* Proximate \& Community\-Oriented in leadership and decision\-making structure (i.e., led by those who intimately understand the problem/opportunity, incorporating impacted communities in decision\-making, led by someone who has direct experience in/with the community served)
* Approaching an inflection point or needing a significant capital inflow to catalyze a differentiated level of impact that they haven’t received support for yet (rather than support for ongoing operations)
* Thoughtfully ambitious ideas that both inspire and convince — have an exciting, breakthrough vision (‘inspire’), clear outcomes, and evidence that the idea will have a significant and concrete impact (‘convince’)

 **Key Responsibilities**

**1\. Set and evolve the sourcing and curation strategy.**

* Advance sourcing and curation strategy for each cycle, forming a clear point of view on where Audacious should be learning, looking, collaborating, and why, ultimately carrying that strategy forward across cycles
* Develop a “future\-oriented” approach by monitoring and anticipating emerging trends and innovations that Audacious actively engages with or responds to
* Actively capture insights and lessons learned to refine and improve Audacious's sourcing and curation activities annually

 **2\. Lead selection and diligence.**

* Apply the sourcing and curation strategy to identify the ideas most worth Audacious's attention \- deciding what advances into the application stage, and bringing along multiple stakeholders and inputs along that decision\-making process
* Lead diligence on selected organizations, rigorously testing readiness, viability, and fit within Audacious's portfolio
* Build the recommendation — evidence, rationale, and risks included — that carries an idea into finalist selection

 **3\. Cultivate and steward the partners who bring ideas to Audacious.**

* Surface strong ideas by building genuine, ongoing relationships with social sector leaders, including funder and intermediary partners (incl. sourcing networks, collaboratives, and others)
* Steward key partner relationships by developing a deep understanding of each stakeholder’s goals, priorities, and aspirations
* Maintain stakeholder relationships continuously, within and across cycles, to ensure trust and rapport among all actors
* Represent Audacious's point of view in the field, using that standing to draw the most ambitious organizations and ideas toward the platform and to create richer opportunities for sector collaboration

 **4\. Build and nurture a team that explores the frontier of audacious social change.**

* Lead the Discovery and Insight vertical and its resourcing strategically, matching capacity to where the real work sits and bringing in the right technical expertise when needed
* Develop people who can source, diligence, and hold partner relationships credibly in their own right — building a team of strong practitioners so Audacious's discovery capacity grows beyond any single person
* Effectively steward relationships and actively collaborate with key Audacious partners (e.g., The Bridgespan Group) with whom we partner year\-round

 **Qualifications** **Career and Functional Experience**

**Essential**

Destination Tomorrow (DT) is a national grassroots LGBTQ\+ organization founded in the Bronx, built on the principle that no individual should have to leave their neighborhood for necessary services. This role will function as an extension of the CEO’s office and play a critical role in helping Destination Tomorrow scale effectively while protecting the CEO’s time and attention. **Supervisor:** Board of Directors

**Type:** Full\-time, 40 hours/week, FMLA\-exempt

Send your resume or CV and a one\-page cover letter to hiring@sonorandesert.org with the subject line “CSDP Executive Director Application."

\*Applications received through Indeed will not be accepted.\*

**About the Coalition for Sonoran Desert Protection**

The Coalition’s mission is to protect the biodiversity of the Sonoran Desert in Southern Arizona through science\-based advocacy, education, and collaboration. We are an alliance of conservation and community organizations that formed in February 1998\. Through our Coalition, we work towards a common vision of a community in the Sonoran Desert where ecosystems are healthy, valued, and protected, with benefits for current and future generations.

**Position description**

The Executive Director provides strategic leadership and day\-to\-day management, ensuring CSDP advances its mission, remains financially sustainable, and operates effectively. As the leader of a small, collaborative team, the Executive Director is both a strategic leader and a working manager, setting organizational direction while remaining actively engaged in programs, advocacy, fundraising, communications, and operations.

The Executive Director serves as the public face of CSDP, building strong relationships with the Board of Directors, staff, coalition members, elected officials, government agencies, land managers, developers, communities, supporters, major donors, and more. Success in this role requires exceptional leadership, sound judgment, strong communication skills, and the ability to navigate complex conservation, land\-use, and local government issues while bringing diverse interests together around a shared vision.

CSDP’s current annual budget is just under $500,000, with anticipated growth in the years ahead. The Executive Director reports to the Board of Directors and is responsible for the leadership and management of CSDP’s staff.

***Core tasks and areas of responsibility***

**Organizational leadership**

* Manage staff and contractors, including hiring, onboarding, performance management, and professional development.
* Maintain and update internal and external policies and procedures, including CSDP’s Employee Handbook.
* Oversee CSDP’s coalition structure and members, including relationships, agreements and contracts, communications, and other elements necessary to maintain a healthy coalition.
* Communicate effectively with the Board.
* Support the Board with organizing meetings, and prepare Board meeting materials.
* Acknowledge and dismantle inequities within CSDP policies, systems, programs, and strategies, while reflecting on lessons learned.
* Foster an organizational culture that embodies CSDP’s core values: collaborative, inclusive, responsible, committed, knowledge\-based, results\-focused, and inspired.

**Financial management**

* Develop and manage CSDP’s annual budget.
* Develop and oversee delivery on CSDP’s fundraising strategy, to include related events and communications.
* Develop and lead CSDP’s foundation and major donor relations.
* Monitor and manage CSDP’s organizational finances, including monthly financial statements.
* Oversee banking, accounting, tax compliance, insurance, payroll, benefits, contracts, and regulatory compliance processes and tasks.

**Policy, planning, and programs**

* Manage CSDP’s Strategic Plan, including regular review and updates.
* Oversee effective implementation and ongoing evaluation of CSDP’s programs.
* Oversee advocacy efforts consistent with CSDP’s priorities.
* Stay informed on relevant and emerging issues.
* Develop a voice for influencing conservation policy and represent CSDP in strategic public forums.
* Develop and maintain effective working relationships and community partnerships with relevant and diverse stakeholders.
* Oversee CSDP’s communications and strategy, including specific publications, branding, public relations, and media engagement.

**Preferred experience and skills**

No candidate is expected to bring every skill, experience, or qualification exactly as listed. If this role excites you and you believe you could make a meaningful contribution to CSDP, we encourage you to apply. We value transferable skills, lived experience, and a willingness to learn alongside demonstrated expertise.

We also strongly encourage women, people of color, LGBTQ\+ individuals, people with disabilities, members of ethnic minorities, foreign\-born residents, and veterans to apply.

We are seeking experience and skills that align with the following:

* 10\+ years of experience in policy, planning, conservation, advocacy, and/or a related field, with a preference for 5\+ years in a management position and direct experience with policy and/or advocacy;

Position Summary


The Executive Director of the Teen Center is a visionary and results\-driven leader responsible for the overall management, strategic direction, growth, and success of the Boys \& Girls Club of Central New Mexico's Teen Center. This position provides leadership to staff, oversees program quality, develops sustainable systems and operational structures, ensures grant compliance, cultivates community partnerships, and creates a positive culture that empowers both youth and staff.


The Executive Director serves as the face of the Teen Center in the community and is responsible for building a high\-performing team dedicated to delivering exceptional youth development experiences. Through strong leadership, delegation, accountability, and collaboration, this position ensures that the Teen Center remains a safe, welcoming, innovative, and engaging environment where teens can achieve academic success, develop workforce readiness skills, strengthen mental wellness, and become leaders in their communities.


This position aligns with the Boys \& Girls Clubs of America (BGCA) Formula for Impact and Program Quality Standards, ensuring programs consistently promote Academic Success, Good Character \& Citizenship, and Healthy Lifestyles.


Essential Duties and Responsibilities


Strategic Leadership \& Vision


* Provide overall leadership and direction for Teen Center operations, programming, and strategic initiatives.
* Develop and implement long\-term goals that align with BGCCNM's mission, strategic plan, and community needs.
* Create and maintain systems that improve organizational efficiency, accountability, communication, and program effectiveness.
* Lead organizational change and continuous improvement efforts.
* Foster a culture of excellence, innovation, teamwork, and youth\-centered decision\-making.
* Ensure all Teen Center operations reflect BGCA's mission, values, and best practices.

Team Development \& Organizational Culture


* Recruit, supervise, coach, and develop a diverse team of skilled professionals.
* Cultivate a positive workplace culture focused on collaboration, accountability, professional growth, and high performance.
* Provide regular supervision, performance evaluations, and professional development opportunities.
* Empower Program Directors and leadership staff through effective delegation and shared ownership of organizational goals.
* Facilitate leadership team meetings and ensure alignment across all program areas.
* Promote clear communication and collaboration among departments.

Program Oversight \& Quality Assurance


* Oversee the development, implementation, and evaluation of all Teen Center programs.
* Ensure programs meet BGCA Program Quality Standards and support Academic Success, Workforce Readiness, Mental Wellness, Character Development, and Leadership Development.
* Collaborate with Program Directors to establish measurable outcomes and program goals.
* Monitor participation, engagement, and retention data to drive continuous improvement.
* Ensure youth voice and feedback are incorporated into program planning and decision\-making.
* Create an engaging, inclusive, and welcoming environment that encourages teen participation and belonging.

Grant Management \& Compliance


* Collaborate with Program Directors and administrative staff to ensure compliance with all grant requirements.
* Oversee collection and reporting of program data, attendance, outcomes, and performance metrics.
* Ensure timely submission of reports for federal, state, local, and private grants.
* Work closely with leadership to identify funding opportunities and support grant development efforts.
* Monitor grant deliverables, budgets, and performance measures.
* Maintain compliance with all funding agency requirements, including 21st Century Community Learning Centers and other youth development funding sources.

Community Relations \& Partnership Development


* Serve as the primary ambassador and spokesperson for the Teen Center.
* Represent Boys \& Girls Club of Central New Mexico at community meetings, public events, schools, networking opportunities, and stakeholder gatherings.
* Develop and maintain strategic partnerships with schools, businesses, government agencies, community organizations, higher education institutions, and mental health providers.
* Advocate for youth and promote awareness of Teen Center programs and services.
* Strengthen relationships with families and community members to increase engagement and support.

Operational Excellence


* Establish and maintain efficient operational systems and procedures.
* Ensure proper management of facilities, equipment, technology, and resources.
* Oversee budgeting and resource allocation in collaboration with organizational leadership.
* Monitor expenditures and ensure responsible stewardship of financial resources.
* Develop systems that maximize staff productivity and operational effectiveness.
* Ensure compliance with organizational policies, BGCA standards, licensing requirements, and safety protocols.

Youth Engagement \& Teen Center Culture


* Foster a Teen Center culture that is safe, inclusive, innovative, and youth\-centered.
* Ensure youth have opportunities to participate in leadership development, workforce readiness, mental health support, academic assistance, and community engagement.
* Promote meaningful relationships between youth and staff.
* Support special events, community service projects, leadership conferences, and youth recognition opportunities.
* Ensure the Teen Center remains responsive to the evolving needs of young people and families.

Education


Bachelor's Degree required in:


* Nonprofit Management
* Education
* Social Work
* Public Administration
* Business

jobs.description:
Grand Valley State University (GVSU) and the Van Andel Institute (VAI) invite applications for a Full Professor and Inaugural Executive Director to lead the newly established AI Research and Innovation Institute (AIRAI), a strategic partnership between GVSU’s College of Computing and the Van Andel Institute.
  

  

The Executive Director will serve as the founding scientific and strategic leader of the AI Research and Innovation Institute, guiding its growth into a globally recognized center of excellence in artificial intelligence. The Full Professor position will be housed in GVSU’s College of Computing, with an adjunct appointment at the Van Andel Institute. Tenure may be offered at hire based on the candidate's qualifications. The Executive Director reports to the Dean of the GVSU College of Computing.
  

  

The successful candidate will be an accomplished scholar and visionary leader with the ability to inspire researchers across disciplines, attract major investments, and build high\-impact collaborations spanning academia, industry, healthcare, and government. We welcome candidates with deep expertise in machine learning (theory or applied), AI systems and infrastructure, computer vision, NLP or multimodal AI, data science and large\-scale AI. The system will allow you to attach up to 5 documents electronically.


If you have questions about the position or the posting, please contact Alison Hodgson hodgsoal@gvsu.edu . If you need technical assistance, please contact Human Resources at hro@gvsu.edu .


**Application Deadline:**

Candidate review will begin the week of September 14, 2026 and continue until the position is filled. This posting may be closed at any time at the discretion of the University.

Grand Rapids is rapidly establishing itself as a national center for healthcare innovation, life sciences, and advanced technologies. The Pet Sustainability Coalition (PSC) is seeking an exceptional Executive Director \& Chief Executive Officer to lead the premier organization advancing sustainability across the global pet industry. jobs.title: Program Director, BEACON
jobs.description:
**About Center for Improving Youth Justice**


The Center for Improving Youth Justice (CIYJ) is a national nonprofit dedicated to improving outcomes for young people in the juvenile justice system. Through Performance\-based Standards (PbS), CIYJ builds performance improvement and accountability into agency, facility, and program operations using a three\-part cycle: collecting data, analyzing performance outcomes and summary reports, and at the heart of PbS using that data to drive improvement and reform. CIYJ's PbS model identifies, monitors, and improves conditions of confinement and treatment services in residential facilities and programs across 22\+ states, grounded in national standards and performance outcome measures. Alongside PbS, CIYJ takes a holistic approach to serving young people and families through mentoring, educational support, and job placement.

 **About Beacon**


BEACON (Building Employment and Career Opportunities Network) is CIYJ's reentry intermediary model, connecting moderate\- to high\-risk justice\-involved youth — including emerging adults who remain under juvenile justice jurisdiction — to education and employment pathways before and after release from residential placement.


Rather than another siloed program, BEACON operates as a neutral backbone that aligns the work of facilities, probation, schools, training providers, employers, and community\-based organizations around a single accountable plan for each young person.


Planning begins no later than 90 days before release and continues through at least 12 months of community follow\-up, across three phases:

* Pre\-Release Preparation — assessment\-informed planning and an individualized education and employment pathway plan.
* Transition — release coordination and immediate activation of school, training, or employment placements.
* Community Reintegration — high\-frequency contact and persistence check\-ins at 30, 90, and 180 days, with coaching through the first year.


In Massachusetts, BEACON's partners include the Massachusetts Department of Youth Services, Massasoit Community College's Future of Work Institute, New England Culinary Arts Training (NECAT), and Haley House, with additional employer and community\-based partners engaged through formal MOUs.

**Position Summary**


The BEACON Program Director is the operational leader of the Massachusetts BEACON initiative, accountable for running the program day to day — from pre\-release enrollment through community reintegration — while building and managing the network of correctional, residential, probation, educational, employer, and community\-based partners that make the model work.


This is a hands\-on operational role: the Program Director carries direct responsibility for enrolling and tracking youth, coordinating with facilities and probation, and ensuring partner commitments are executed, not just an oversight function.


In Year 1, the Program Director may also carry a direct service caseload of Youth Opportunity Advocacy work in addition to program leadership, transitioning fully into leadership and supervision as the Massachusetts team grows.

**Program Operations \& Reentry Coordination**

* Own end\-to\-end execution of the BEACON model in Massachusetts: pre\-release planning, release\-day transition coordination, and community reintegration through at least 12 months of follow\-up.
* Conduct or oversee intake and enrollment using validated risk and needs assessment tools, in coordination with correctional and probation partners, beginning no later than 90 days before release.
* Cultivate referral pipelines from community residential programs and probation departments as an additional source of eligible youth and emerging adults, ensuring the enrollment pipeline is not limited to secure residential release alone.
* Broker enrollment with schools and training providers, match youth to fair\-chance employers and apprenticeships, and administer scholarships and transitional employment stipends through CIYJ's Education and Employment Foundation.
* Ensure persistence monitoring and check\-ins occur on schedule at 30, 90, and 180 days post\-release, and drive corrective action when a young person's plan stalls.
* Deliver a true warm handoff to community\-based organizations for wraparound supports, including housing, mental health, and transportation.

**Partnership Development \& Management**

* Correctional \& Probation Agencies — maintain pre\-release access agreements, serve as the designated staff liaison, and manage data sharing that supports case planning and outcome tracking.
* Community Residential Programs \& Probation Departments — build and maintain referral relationships with community\-based residential programs and probation offices as an ongoing intake source, ensuring youth and emerging adults supervised in these settings have equal access to BEACON pathways.
* Educational Institutions — manage expedited enrollment pathways, coordinate financial aid navigation, and ensure attendance verification and reporting are current.
* Employer Partners — recruit and vet employers, negotiate entry\-level positions at an agreed wage floor, and monitor fair\-chance hiring practices and outcome tracking.
* Community\-Based Organizations — manage referral relationships, coordinate with CIYJ case managers on family engagement, and maintain the wraparound service network.
* Negotiate, execute, and maintain formal MOUs with all Massachusetts partners, ensuring each agreement reflects current program needs and is actively followed.
* Represent CIYJ and BEACON with existing and prospective Massachusetts partners, including site visits, partner meetings, and outreach to expand the network as the program grows.

**Performance, Data \& Accountability**

* Maintain accurate, timely data on participation and outcomes across education, employment, stability, and justice\-system domains, feeding CIYJ's real\-time dashboards.
