jobs.title: CEO
jobs.description:
\#\#\# Lead with Purpose. Build the Future.

For nearly \*\*50 years\*\*, the \*\*Dr. Piper Center\*\* has been changing lives across Southwest Florida by empowering older adults to serve their communities while supporting frail seniors, at\-risk youth, and children with special needs. Our mission is to enhance the social and economic well\-being of adults 55\+ through innovative programs that strengthen our entire community.

We're entering an exciting new chapter and are looking for an energetic, visionary leader who is ready to build on our legacy while creating the future.

Reporting directly to the Board of Directors, the CEO will lead a dedicated team, oversee day\-to\-day operations, strengthen our financial sustainability, expand community partnerships, and cultivate an exceptional workplace culture.

If you're equally passionate about people, fundraising, grants, strategy, and leadership—we'd love to meet you.

\#\#\# What You'll Do

As our CEO, you will:

\* Inspire, mentor, and develop a high\-performing, collaborative team

\* Create and maintain a positive, healthy organizational culture built on trust and accountability

\* Lead strategic planning and organizational growth

\* Oversee finances, budgeting, compliance, and daily operations

\* Research, write, and manage federal, state, local, and foundation grants

\* Develop new funding opportunities and strengthen donor relationships

\* Work closely with an engaged Board of Directors

\* Represent the organization throughout Southwest Florida as our primary spokesperson  
\* Build meaningful partnerships with community leaders, government agencies, businesses, and philanthropic organizations

\* Measure outcomes and ensure our programs continue making a lasting community impact

\#\#\# The Leader We're Looking For

We're seeking someone who:

\* Has nonprofit executive or senior leadership experience

\*Has demonstrated success writing, securing, and managing grants

\* Understands nonprofit finance, budgeting, and compliance

\* Has fundraising and donor development experience

\* Builds positive workplace cultures where employees thrive

\* Is an exceptional communicator who enjoys engaging with community partners and stakeholders

\* Can think strategically while remaining hands\-on when needed\* Leads with integrity, compassion, transparency, and optimism

\#\#\# Preferred Qualifications  
\* Bachelor's degree required; Master's degree or MBA preferred  
\* Five or more years of nonprofit leadership experience  
\* Grant writing and grant administration expertise  
\* Experience working with nonprofit Boards of Directors  
\* Strong financial management and budgeting skills  
\* Excellent written and verbal communication skills

\#\#\# Why Join the Dr. Piper Center?

You'll have the opportunity to lead an organization with nearly five decades of community impact while helping shape its future.

We offer:

\* Competitive salary ($80,000–$100,000 DOE)  
\* Employer\-paid medical, dental, and vision insurance  
\* 401(k) with employer match  
\* Generous paid time off and holidays  
\* A supportive Board committed to organizational success  
\* The opportunity to make a meaningful difference in the lives of seniors and families throughout Southwest Florida

If you're an experienced nonprofit leader who believes great organizations begin with great culture, strong partnerships, and sustainable funding, we'd love to hear from you.

\*\*Apply today and help lead the Dr. Piper Center into its next 50 years of service.\*\*

Pay: $80,000\.00 \- $100,000\.00 per year

jobs.title: Senior Director of Operations
jobs.description:
**About Journey Found:**  

Headquartered in Manchester, CT, and established in 2013, Journey Found (JF) is a proven leader in providing behavioral, residential, and many other support services to Connecticut individuals with Pervasive Developmental Disorders and Intellectual Disabilities, enabling them to become all they are capable of being. Journey Found currently serves 73 adults with intellectual disabilities ranging in age from 21\-82 in small (mostly 3\-person) group homes and apartments, in individualized day support services, and a growing number in their own or their family’s homes. Services are person\-centered, and each person has an individual plan of service that addresses their hopes, wants, and needs in each aspect of their life: home, work, recreation, medical, and sometimes education.  

Journey Found’s annual budget is $19 million, 95% of which comes from contracts with the state of Connecticut. The organization has 292 employees (\~166 FTEs) and is governed by an actively engaged 10\-member board.

 **Opportunity Going Forward:**  

The Senior Director of Operations provides oversight of all the agency's operations and acts as the internal face of the organization. This role is critical in helping fulfill Journey Found’s mission of providing the best person\-centered services to people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and to secure its sustainability in service to this mission.  

The Senior Director of Operations is a dynamic leader and creative individual who works to maintain and expand the high\-quality services provided by the organization. The candidate is willing to learn and remains flexible enough to adjust priorities to ensure the organization continues to fulfill its mission as new service trends emerge, and changes in the environment occur. Journey Found’s next Senior Director of Operations will join the team at an exciting time. The field is changing, with innovations in technology presenting new ways to serve the people Journey Found supports. The new Senior Director of Operations will have the opportunity to work with the CEO to explore and realize the impact of new service models that align with Journey Found’s person\-centered culture, leaving an imprint on the organization and the lives it touches. In addition, there is a strong team in place, and the new Senior Director of Operations will be able to partner with team leaders who have been providing high\-quality services to the people JF serves while looking for growth opportunities within the organization.  

The Senior Director of Operations reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer under the general direction of the Board of Directors. The position is based in JF’s Manchester, CT office and is expected to be on\-site for the majority of the Monday\-Friday work week, with the flexibility to work remotely as their schedule and commitments permit. This individual has direct supervisory responsibilities for five professionals in the role of Director of Support Services, the Facilities Manager, and the Director of Clinical Services. This role indirectly supervises the organization’s nursing department and approximately 250 Direct Support Professionals.




**The Senior Director of Operations Key Responsibilities Include:**

 **Management and Administration**  

* Foster a work culture of success, empowerment, team building, and reward organizational effectiveness.
* Effectively resolve conflicts, deal with challenging situations, and make smart decisions.
* Assure compliance with federal, state, and local regulations, statutes, licensure, accreditation (where applicable), and agency policy.
* Provide on\-call support to the organization.
* Critically evaluate situations and strategically plan solutions.
* Firmly, promptly, and effectively manage crisis situations.
* Adeptly collect and analyze data to inform decisions that lead to positive outcomes.
**Organizational Engagement**  

* Make strong connections with the staff and people supported.
* Strongly focus on stakeholder engagement.
* Effectively communicate with families of people supported.
* Approach working with teams and mediating positive outcomes in a proactive manner.
**Clinical Oversight**  

* Partner with the Director of Clinical Services to ensure high\-quality services are provided.
* Ensure the Clinical Team completes continuing education to promote learning and best practice approaches to clinical supports.
* Effectuate a Person\-Centered approach using applied behavior analysis (ABA) techniques and strategies.
**Facilities Oversight**  

* Develop and evaluate proactive facilities management plans.
* Ensure all Journey Found homes are maintained to the highest standards, and they reflect positively upon their communities.
* Manage a network of vendors and property managers in support of Journey Found’s mission.
* Negotiate contractual agreements and manage capital improvement projects.
* Maximize the efforts of the Facilities department by triaging work orders.



**Desired Credentials/Profile of the Ideal Candidate:**  

There are innumerable ways to learn, grow, and excel professionally with Journey Found. We respect this when we review applications and take a broad look at the experience of each applicant. We want to get to know you and the unique strengths you will bring to the work. We will most likely be interested in your candidacy if you demonstrate most of the qualifications and attributes listed below:  

* A sincere commitment to Journey Found’s mission of delivering high\-quality, person\-centered services to people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
* Five or more years of managerial and administrative responsibilities in a non\-profit organization or an equivalent combination of training and experience.
* At least three years of experience working with the CT Department of Developmental Services (DDS).
* An established track record of fostering a successful and effective team\-base

The Chief Philanthropy Officer provides leadership to the organization’s efforts to obtain financial and other support to sustain its work immediately and for the long term, in cooperation with our active, fundraising board and CEO.

Collaborate with the communications manager and the volunteer staff to create and promote fund raising and philanthropic opportunities within all volunteer programs and communication materials.


7\.*Agency Representation* \- Make presentations as appropriate on behalf of the organization.

	+ Lead and guide the Director of real estate development in sourcing, underwriting, and executing new investment opportunities.
	+ Review and approve major acquisition and investment recommendations, ensuring alignment with company objectives, risk tolerance, and long\-term company strategy and present qualified opportunities to Forza CFO and Rehab Industries Board.
  


* **Construction Oversight (Director of Construction)**
	+ Provide leadership, oversight, and accountability for the Director of Construction across both pre\- and post\-acquisition development activities.
	+ Ensure construction and development projects are delivered on time, within budget, and in accordance with established quality standards.
	+ Oversee capital improvement planning and execution across the entire real estate portfolio.
  


* **Property Management Oversight (Property Management Team)**
	+ Provide direct oversight of the Property Management team, ensuring all properties operate within budget, remain stabilized, and maintain positive cash flow.
	+ Partner with the CFO to monitor property\-level financial performance against established goals and implement corrective actions as needed.
  


* **Cross Department Coordination \& Communication**
	+ Facilitate effective communication and collaboration among Finance, Acquisitions, Construction, Property Management, and Legal.
	+ Lead regular executive and departmental meetings to review progress, identify risks, and prioritize initiatives.
	+ Promote a collaborative, results\-driven culture that supports the company’s mission, vision, and long\-term goals.

jobs.title: Chief Philanthropy Officer
jobs.description:
**Chief Philanthropy Officer**

**About The Virginia Home**

The mission of The Virginia Home is rooted in the belief that every individual deserves a life filled with purpose, connection, and respect. Founded more than a century ago through a bold vision to provide compassionate, lifelong care for individuals with disabilities, The Virginia Home has long been a place of dignity, innovation, and community. As we embark on our most ambitious capital campaign to date to build a new, innovative campus, we are creating more than a space, we are shaping a future where residents can experience greater independence, enriched programming, and a vibrant community designed around their needs. This moment marks a transformative step forward in honoring our residents and expanding our impact for generations to come.

**Position Summary**

The Chief Philanthropy Officer (CPO) serves as the senior fundraising leader for The Virginia Home and is responsible for the strategy, execution, and performance of all philanthropic revenue in support of the organization’s mission and capital campaign. This role provides overall leadership for the advancement function, including major and principal gifts, annual giving, campaign strategy, and donor engagement.

Working in close partnership with the CEO, Board leadership, and campaign consultants, the CPO will lead the execution of a transformational capital campaign while building a high\-performing, metrics\-driven fundraising team and culture.

**Duties/Responsibilities:**

**Campaign Leadership \& Strategy**

* Lead execution of the organization’s capital campaign strategy in partnership with the CEO and Board leadership.
* Finalize and refine campaign goals, timeline, and key milestones.
* Ensure alignment between campaign priorities, annual fundraising goals, and donor strategy.
* Drive overall campaign momentum, pacing, and accountability.

**Principal Gift Leadership**

* Maintain a personal portfolio of the organization’s high\-capacity donors, including individuals, foundations, and corporations.
* Help develop strategy for Chief Executive Officer’s engagement with top\-level prospects and donors.
* Lead strategy and solicitation for principal gifts ($500K\+), including joint donor engagement with CEO, Board members and senior volunteers.
* Serve as the organization’s lead fundraiser for transformational gifts for both the annual fund and campaign utilizing all giving vehicles, including planned giving.

**Team Leadership \& Accountability**

* Provide direct supervision and leadership to philanthropy staff.
* Establish and reinforce clear portfolio management expectations, performance metrics, and accountability systems.
* Lead weekly team meetings focused on pipeline progress, strategy, and donor movement.
* Facilitate monthly one\-on\-one meetings with all direct reports to review metric performance and strategy.
* Foster a results\-oriented, collaborative, and donor\-centered team culture.

**Board \& Volunteer Engagement**

* Partner with the Campaign Cabinet and Board leadership to activate volunteer fundraising efforts.
* Prepare and support Board members and senior volunteers for cultivation and solicitation opportunities.
* Serve as a key liaison between staff and volunteer leadership to advance campaign goals.

**Revenue Oversight \& Reporting**

* Oversee all fundraising revenue and ensure alignment with organizational and campaign goals.
* Track campaign progress and ensure clear, consistent reporting on pipeline, solicitations, and closed gifts.
* Provide monthly fundraising updates to executive leadership and the Board.
* Utilize data to inform strategy, prioritize opportunities, and forecast results.

**Preferred Attributes**

* Proven ability to lead high\-performing fundraising teams.
* Strong presence and ability to engage effectively with Board members and senior stakeholders.
* Highly strategic with strong execution discipline.
* Collaborative, results\-oriented, and mission\-driven.

**Education and Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree required.
* 10\+ years of progressive fundraising experience, including senior leadership roles.
* Demonstrated success leading major and principal gift fundraising efforts, including securing seven\-figure gifts.
* Experience leading or participating in a capital campaign.
* Strong leadership, strategic thinking, and relationship management skills.

*The Virginia Home provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local laws.*

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $175,000\.00 \- $181,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 403(b)
* 403(b) matching
* Dental insurance
* Dependent health insurance coverage
* Employee assistance program
* Health insurance
* Health savings account
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Professional development assistance
* Vision insurance

Education:

* Bachelor's (Required)

Experience:

* Fundraising: 10 years (Required)

Ability to Relocate:

* Richmond, VA 23220: Relocate before starting work (Required)

Work Location: In person

* Collaborate with other Penn State units, departments, or Center leadership to develop strategic partnerships, advance the mission of research initiatives and projects, and develop long\-term plans for resource allocation
* Work with students, colleagues, and partners to research, develop, and implement digital scholarship and publishing projects in the Center

jobs.description:
**Job Posting Title:**


Director of Development, Major Gifts**\-**

**Hiring Department:**


Texas Memorial Museum**\-**

**Position Open To:**


All Applicants**\-**

**Weekly Scheduled Hours:**


40**\-**

**FLSA Status:**


Exempt from FLSA**\-**

**Earliest Start Date:**


Aug 01, 2026**\-**

**Position Duration:**


Expected to Continue**\-**

**Location:**


UT MAIN CAMPUS**\-**

**Job Details:**

**General Notes**
-----------------


Texas Science \& Natural History Museum is developing new exhibits, programs, and experiences that spark curiosity, promote belonging, and tell stories about Texas science and natural history. Since reopening in September 2023, the reimagined institution blends its historic building and world\-class collections with engaging, modern exhibits focused on science education. The museum has welcomed more than 200,000 visitors from all 50 states and 28 countries while supporting preK–12 education through field trips and outreach and presenting an active schedule of public programs. The museum continues to evolve as a vibrant educational and cultural hub with a strong path toward long\-term sustainability.

**Purpose**
-----------


To serve as the Director of Development, Major Gifts, on the museum’s development team. The museum seeks a seasoned fundraising professional to identify and cultivate new donor prospects with a capacity of $100,000\+. Reporting to the Director of Development, the position holds a critical role advancing Texas Science \& Natural Museum’s strategic priorities through fundraising.

**Responsibilities**
--------------------

* Build and carry an active portfolio of 40\-60 well\-qualified prospective donors with a capacity of $100,000\+ and solicit gifts to support operations, capital projects, education programs, and endowments through pledged and planned monetary gifts. Engage in strategy, identification, qualification, cultivation, relationship building, and closing of major gifts to support the museum’s priorities.
* Participate in goal setting, proposal preparation, strategy development, and stewardship as required for donors in the managed portfolio and as requested.
* Successfully and consistently deliver funding proposals with a focus on engaging new donors and building contributed revenue that is available within the fiscal year as well as building endowment funding.
* Maintain an understanding of the museum’s priorities, vision, and mission. Effectively work with, communicate with, and collaborate with leadership and other staff to develop funding opportunities to reach annual budgetary and long\-term goals. Work collaboratively, proactively, and in a professional, service\-oriented manner with all departments and development staff to further goals and mission of the museum.
* Utilize fundraising databases and prospecting tools. Maintain appropriate electronic documentation on a timely basis, including engagement activities, proposal tracking, contact reports, donor strategies, and forecasting.
* Work collaboratively and proactively with Texas Development and development colleagues within the College of Natural Sciences to align and advance the museum’s fundraising efforts. Leverage relationships with development officers across the university to explore and/or strengthen fundraising opportunities for the museum. Participate in professional development opportunities to create and nurture a culture of philanthropy within Texas Science \& Natural History Museum and its constituencies.
* Performs related duties as assigned. May work on assigned projects.
**Required Qualifications**
---------------------------

* Bachelor’s Degree and minimum of 5\+ years experience in major gift fundraising
* Proven success in developing and executing full\-cycle donor strategies
* Strong emotional intelligence, intellectual curiosity, and strategic thinking
* Excellent communication skills (written, verbal, interpersonal).
* Effective project management and problem\-solving abilities, thrives under pressure
* Collaborative and independent work style; able to engage across teams
* Proficient in CRM platforms such as VIP, Salesforce, or similar applications
* Willingness and ability to travel to engage with prospects and donors
* Demonstrated discretion, integrity, and professionalism in all interactions
* Relevant education and experience may be substituted as appropriate
**Preferred Qualifications**
----------------------------

* 10\+ years in major gift fundraising
* Prior experience at UT or in a large, complex academic research university environment
* Background in fundraising within a museum or educational organization
* Graduate degree and/or Certified Fund Raising Executive (CFRE) certification
**Salary Range**
----------------


$115,000\+ depending on qualifications

**Working Conditions**
----------------------


Hybrid position

**Required Materials**
----------------------

* Resume/CV
* 3 work references with their contact information; at least one reference should be from a supervisor
* Letter of interest

**Important** **for applicants who are NOT current university employees or contingent workers:** You will be prompted to submit your resume the first time you apply, then you will be provided an option to upload a new Resume for subsequent applications. Any additional Required Materials (letter of interest, references, etc.) will be uploaded in the Application Questions section; you will be able to multi\-select additional files. Before submitting your online job application, ensure that ALL Required Materials have been uploaded. Once your job application has been submitted, you cannot make changes.

**Important for Current university employees and contingent workers:** As a current university employee or contingent worker, you MUST apply within Workday by searching for Find UT Jobs. If you are a current University employee, log\-in to Workday, navigate to your Worker Profile, click the Career link in the left hand navigation

jobs.title: Director of Philanthropy (Immediate Opening)
jobs.description:
**Description**


**Director of Philanthropy**

**Role Mission:** The Director of Philanthropy is a seasoned major\-gifts fundraiser responsible for securing transformational philanthropic investments that advance IDEA Public Schools’ mission. Managing a multi\-region portfolio of high\-capacity donors, the Director drives strategic cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship to grow IDEA’s pipeline of individuals, corporations, and foundations capable of giving $10,000–$250,000\+ annually.  

  

Operating within IDEA’s new Foundation model, the Director applies a disciplined, data\-informed approach to portfolio strategy—executing thoughtful qualification, crafting tailored donor pathways, and aligning every engagement to long\-term revenue growth. The role requires mastery in major gifts, including multi\-year commitments, donor\-advised funds, endowed gifts, and planned giving, and the ability to translate IDEA’s bold ambitions into compelling investment opportunities.  

  

A trusted philanthropic advisor to donors and a strategic thought partner across IDEA’s fundraising and regional leadership, the Director collaborates closely with program leaders, regional executives, Advancement Services, and the Donor Experience team to design high\-impact proposals, stewardship plans, and engagement experiences that deepen trust and strengthen IDEA’s financial sustainability.  

  

Reporting to the Lead Director of Philanthropy, this role bridges strategy and execution—ensuring IDEA’s major gifts work is coordinated, relationship\-centered, metrics\-aligned, and high\-impact. The Director plays a critical frontline role in expanding IDEA’s community of loyal supporters and securing the philanthropic investments needed to serve more students, in more regions, for years to come


**Supervisory Responsibilities:** None

**Location:** This is a full\-time remote position based in Texas, with preference given to candidates who live in Austin, El Paso, Houston, Permian Basin (Midland/Odessa), Rio Grande Valley, San Antonio, and Tarrant County (Fort Worth), or who are willing to relocate.

**Travel Expectations:** This role requires regular in\-person donor meetings across multiple regions, including travel within and outside the Director’s home market. Approximately 30–40% travel annually, with increased volume during peak fundraising periods (e.g., donor solicitations, cultivation events, College Signing Day, and the Annual Gala).

**What You’ll Do – Accountabilities**

**Essential Duties:**


* Manage a multi\-region portfolio of high\-capacity individual, corporate, and foundation donors, leading strategic cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship efforts that secure major gifts of $10,000–$250,000\+ annually.
* Develop and execute individualized donor strategies informed by data, research, and relationship history to advance prospects through the major gifts pipeline toward long\-term philanthropic investment.
* Conduct thoughtful donor qualification and discovery to assess capacity, interest, and readiness, aligning donor priorities with IDEA’s funding needs and strategic initiatives.
* Prepare and deliver compelling proposals and gift solicitations, including multi\-year commitments, donor\-advised fund gifts, endowed gifts, and planned giving opportunities.
* Maintain accurate, timely documentation of donor interactions, strategies, and progress within IDEA’s CRM to support effective portfolio management, forecasting, and collaboration.
* Collaborate with internal partners to design and execute stewardship plans, engagement experiences, and donor communications that demonstrate impact, reinforce trust, and encourage continued investment.

 **Additional Duties and Responsibilities:**


In addition to the essential duties outlined above, the Director of Philanthropy contributes to portfolio planning, forecasting, and continuous improvement efforts related to major gifts fundraising. This role participates in special initiatives, pilots, or campaigns that support the effectiveness and scalability of IDEA’s major gifts program; supports leadership reporting and analysis as needed; and maintains flexibility to adapt strategies in response to donor feedback, regional priorities, and organizational needs.

 **Knowledge and Skills – Competencies**

* **Make Strategic Decisions:** This team member makes informed decisions by gathering reliable information, asking clarifying questions, and evaluating multiple options. They thoughtfully align their choices with team objectives, current responsibilities, and the broader mission of the organization.
* **Manage Work and Teams:** This team member sets clear, measurable goals and regularly reflects on progress, adjusting actions as needed. They prioritize work aligned with their goals using a task management system and consistently meet deadlines through effective time management.
* **Grow Self and Others:** This team member demonstrates self\-awareness by actively seeking and thoughtfully responding to feedback with curiosity and a growth mindset. They identify development areas, implement feedback when appropriate, and pursue opportunities to build their knowledge and skills.
* **Build a Culture of Trust:** This team member proactively builds strong personal and professional relationships with individual stakeholders and regularly seeks feedback to improve their work experience. They create a supportive environment where others feel safe to take risks and learn from mistakes without fear of retribution.
* **Communicate Deliberately:** This team member communicates thoughtfully by anticipating potential misunderstandings and providing necessary context to ensure clarity. They leverage structured communication channels to address challenges, ask meaningful questions, and guide conversations toward solutions, while actively listening to the concerns of others.

  



**Additional Skills:**

* Proven ability to cultivate, solicit, and close gifts of $10,000

Approximately 30–40% travel annually, with increased volume during peak fundraising periods (e.g., donor solicitations, cultivation events, College Signing Day, and the Annual Gala).

**What You’ll Do – Accountabilities**

**Essential Duties:**


* Manage a multi\-region portfolio of high\-capacity individual, corporate, and foundation donors, leading strategic cultivation, solicitation, and stewardship efforts that secure major gifts of $10,000–$250,000\+ annually.
* Develop and execute individualized donor strategies informed by data, research, and relationship history to advance prospects through the major gifts pipeline toward long\-term philanthropic investment.
* Conduct thoughtful donor qualification and discovery to assess capacity, interest, and readiness, aligning donor priorities with IDEA’s funding needs and strategic initiatives.
* Prepare and deliver compelling proposals and gift solicitations, including multi\-year commitments, donor\-advised fund gifts, endowed gifts, and planned giving opportunities.
* Maintain accurate, timely documentation of donor interactions, strategies, and progress within IDEA’s CRM to support effective portfolio management, forecasting, and collaboration.
* Collaborate with internal partners to design and execute stewardship plans, engagement experiences, and donor communications that demonstrate impact, reinforce trust, and encourage continued investment.

 **Additional Duties and Responsibilities:**


In addition to the essential duties outlined above, the Director of Philanthropy contributes to portfolio planning, forecasting, and continuous improvement efforts related to major gifts fundraising. This role participates in special initiatives, pilots, or campaigns that support the effectiveness and scalability of IDEA’s major gifts program; supports leadership reporting and analysis as needed; and maintains flexibility to adapt strategies in response to donor feedback, regional priorities, and organizational needs.

 **Knowledge and Skills – Competencies**

* **Make Strategic Decisions:** This team member makes informed decisions by gathering reliable information, asking clarifying questions, and evaluating multiple options. They thoughtfully align their choices with team objectives, current responsibilities, and the broader mission of the organization.
* **Manage Work and Teams:** This team member sets clear, measurable goals and regularly reflects on progress, adjusting actions as needed. They prioritize work aligned with their goals using a task management system and consistently meet deadlines through effective time management.
* **Grow Self and Others:** This team member demonstrates self\-awareness by actively seeking and thoughtfully responding to feedback with curiosity and a growth mindset. They identify development areas, implement feedback when appropriate, and pursue opportunities to build their knowledge and skills.
* **Build a Culture of Trust:** This team member proactively builds strong personal and professional relationships with individual stakeholders and regularly seeks feedback to improve their work experience. They create a supportive environment where others feel safe to take risks and learn from mistakes without fear of retribution.
* **Communicate Deliberately:** This team member communicates thoughtfully by anticipating potential misunderstandings and providing necessary context to ensure clarity. They leverage structured communication channels to address challenges, ask meaningful questions, and guide conversations toward solutions, while actively listening to the concerns of others.

  



**Additional Skills:**

* Proven ability to cultivate, solicit, and close gifts of $10,000

**Job Title:** Managing Director
**Department:** Growth and Development
**Location:** Remote in the San Franciso Bay Area
**Who We Are**
Today’s societal challenges are more complex, interconnected, and urgent than ever. Sunflower Services is comprised of over 240 professionals with deep expertise in the philanthropic sector. We provide support services to fiscal sponsors and intermediaries—and their hundreds of mission\-driven projects—that are creating meaningful change. We welcome jobseekers who are motivated by results and driven by impact. For a list of current job openings at Sunflower, please visit our LinkedIn page.
Sunflower Services is an independent, nonprofit\-owned Public Benefit Corporation dedicated to providing operational excellence to nonprofit and social impact organizations. Sunflower delivers integrated solutions in finance, HR, grants management, compliance, and other administrative areas, helping mission\-driven organizations and changemakers focus on what matters most: their impact.
**Position Overview**Sunflower Services is seeking a bold, relationship\-driven Managing Director of Business Development to help shape the next chapter of the growth of a newly formed ecosystem providing fiscal sponsorship to changemakers tackling today's thorniest problems.
We are a private benefit corporation, wholly owned by three 501c3 public charities: New Venture Fund, Windward Fund, and Hopewell Fund. This is a senior leadership role for someone who combines deep fluency in the social sector with an entrepreneurial mindset and a track record of building high\-value, trust\-based partnerships.
The ideal candidate brings firsthand knowledge of the fiscal sponsorship landscape and understands how philanthropic capital flows, how changemakers and their funders make structural decisions, and how to position this ecosystem, powered by Sunflower’s unparalleled services, as the partner of choice for organizations operating at the frontier of social change. jobs.title: Development Director
jobs.description:
Best Beginnings is a public\-private partnership that mobilizes people and resources to ensure all Alaska children begin school ready to succeed. Through partnerships, early literacy programs, family engagement, and public awareness efforts, Best Beginnings connects children and families with the resources they need to build a strong foundation for lifelong learning.

Best Beginnings is seeking a relationship\-focused Development Director to lead the organization's fundraising efforts. Working closely with the Executive Director, this position is responsible for building meaningful relationships with donors, securing philanthropic support, and implementing fundraising strategies that sustain and grow the organization's impact across Alaska.

This is a hands\-on fundraising position. The successful candidate will spend the majority of their time cultivating donors, managing fundraising campaigns, securing corporate sponsorships, and strengthening a culture of philanthropy throughout the organization.

The Development Director reports directly to the Executive Director. The position provides leadership to fundraising volunteers and serves as staff liaison to the Board Fund Development Committee.

This is a full\-time (40 hours per week), exempt position.

Work location: hybrid (approximately 25\-35% in person)

Some evening and weekend work is required for donor meetings, community events, and fundraising activities. Occasional travel within Alaska may be required.

**TASKS AND RESPONSIBILITIES** 

**Individual Giving \& Major Gifts (40%)**

* Build, cultivate, solicit, and steward relationships with individual donors and major gift prospects.
* Manage a portfolio of current and prospective donors through all stages of the donor cycle.
* Conduct donor visits and prepare proposals, gift agreements, and stewardship materials.
* Develop personalized cultivation and recognition strategies that encourage long\-term giving.
* Coordinate donor meetings involving the Executive Director and Board members.
* Conduct prospect research and maintain an active pipeline of future donors.

**Fundraising Campaigns \& Partnerships (30%)**

* Implement Best Beginnings' annual fundraising plan in partnership with the Executive Director.
* Lead annual giving campaigns, including year\-end giving, Giving Tuesday, Pick.Click.Give., monthly giving, direct mail, and other fundraising initiatives.
* Cultivate and steward corporate sponsors and community partners.
* Develop sponsorship opportunities and recognition plans.
* Collaborate with staff and contractors to create fundraising appeals, donor stories, digital campaigns, and marketing materials.
* Evaluate campaign performance and recommend improvements.

**Donor Database Management (10%)**

* Ensure timely donor acknowledgment and stewardship activities.
* Oversee the integrity of donor records, gift tracking, and reporting in the organization's donor database.
* Work with administrative staff responsible for gift entry and routine database maintenance.
* Prepare fundraising reports and donor analyses.
* Monitor fundraising progress toward annual goals.

**Special Events \& Volunteer Engagement (10%)**

* Provide staff leadership for fundraising events and volunteer event committees.
* Coordinate sponsorship fulfillment and donor engagement associated with fundraising events.
* Work collaboratively with staff to recruit, support, and recognize volunteers assisting with fundraising activities.
* Collaborate with community partners to expand fundraising opportunities.

**Development Planning \& Board Support (10%)**

* Staff the Board Fund Development Committee.
* Prepare committee agendas, reports, and supporting materials.
* Support Board members in donor cultivation and fundraising efforts.
* Stay current on fundraising trends and recommend strategies that strengthen Best Beginnings' long\-term sustainability.

**QUALIFICATIONS**

**Education/Work Experience**

* Bachelor's degree in nonprofit management, communications, business, marketing, public administration, or a related field, or equivalent professional experience.
* Three or more years of progressively responsible experience in fundraising, development, advancement, sales, business development, or relationship management preferred.
* Demonstrated success cultivating relationships and achieving fundraising or revenue goals.

**Skills, Knowledge, and Attitudes**

* Passion for improving outcomes for Alaska's young children and families.
* Excellent relationship\-building and interpersonal skills.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills.
* Comfortable making direct fundraising asks and building long\-term donor relationships.
* Highly organized with excellent project management skills.
* Experience working with boards, volunteers, and community partners.
* Experience using donor management software or CRM systems; Bloomerang experience is a plus.
* Knowledge of Alaska communities and appreciation for the state's geographic and cultural diversity.
* Valid Alaska driver's license and access to reliable transportation for local meetings.

**BENEFITS**

* Flexible work schedule
* Holiday pay and prorated paid time off (PTO)
* Employer retirement match (up to 3%)
* Monthly wellness stipend ($300 per month)
* Internet \& Phone reimbursement ($80 per month)
* Professional development opportunities

**COMPENSATION**

* $75,000 \- $82,000 starting salary

**TO APPLY** 

Please email (with “Development Director” in the subject line), or mail a letter of interest and resume to:

Amie Collins, Executive Director

911 W. 8th Ave., Ste. 101, PMB 79, Anchorage, AK 99501

Pay: $75,000\.00 \- $82,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 403(b) matching
* Flexible schedule
* Paid time off

Work Location: Hybrid remote in Anchorage, AK 99507

JOB DESCRIPTION
External Relations at the University of Virginia (UVA) is seeking a highly qualified candidate to fill the role of **Director of Development** (Community Hospitals). The University of Virginia has a strong and reputable advancement community, boasting effective, results\-oriented, and resourceful professionals. Our community comprises a robust central advancement organization, 12 University schools, and more than 25 University\-related programs. We offer a wide variety of career opportunities, as well as an outstanding environment for professional growth and development. This is a remarkable opportunity to contribute to building a best\-in\-class fundraising program in the Northern Piedmont and Northern Virginia markets in support of UVA Health.

**Position Summary**


UVA Health owns and operates three community hospitals, UVA Health Prince William Medical Center, UVA Health Haymarket Medical Center, and UVA Health Culpeper Medical Center, and several clinics including UVA Health Cancer Center Gainesville. The Director of Development is charged with supporting a sustainable and successful fund\-raising program for these community hospitals and clinics and serving as the primary in\-market resource for friends, donors, and volunteers.


Reporting to the Executive Director, UVA Community Health Foundation and working closely with UVA Health leaders, the Director has a unique opportunity to further develop this program, building a culture of community philanthropy that prioritizes major gifts. The Director of Development is responsible for implementing a strategy and building a pipeline that will grow to consistently produce gifts over $100k and being accountable for development outcomes by documenting and reporting on individual and team metrics.


Preferred candidates will have previous experience fundraising within higher education or a healthcare setting. They will demonstrate entrepreneurship and creativity in identifying prospects. A compelling candidate will have the desire to establish a foundation of excellence and will possess a proven record of meeting and exceeding goals and improving outcomes. They will also have demonstrated leadership skills and be able to create a highly stable and effective team by articulating clear goals and responsibilities and by providing feedback to support individual accountability.

**Responsibilities**

* Serve as the lead in\-market fundraiser for UVA Health managing a portfolio of donors and prospects. Identify, cultivate, solicit and steward these individuals in support of the mission and priorities of program leadership.
* In cooperation with the Executive Director, develop and execute a comprehensive fundraising strategy for UVA Community Health pulling from multiple prospect populations including patient families, community members, UVA friends and alumni, and beyond while serving as the lead fundraiser for community members and friends/alumni.
* Create and implement a transparent and intentional donor strategy to move prospective donors along the development continuum. Cultivate new and existing donors to higher levels of support for the departments and programs of oversight, including research and care of patients.

* Collaborate with UVA Health Development Office (UVAHD) colleagues to assure alignment and synergy in fundraising efforts. Work in tandem and in a donor\-centric manner to advance UVA Health broadly.

* Develop and maintain an informed layperson's understanding of the science behind the care delivered in\-market broadly. Act as a translator and synthesizer of ideas when needed to enhance communication for prospects/donors.
* Build and strengthen relationships with UVA and UVA Health leadership and key stakeholders as needed.
* Other duties as assigned.

  

  

The ideal candidate possesses excellent interpersonal skills, emphasizing collaboration, teamwork, and a solution\-oriented mindset, with exceptional oral and written communication abilities to convey information clearly across varying audiences. They demonstrate a strong customer service orientation, ensuring client satisfaction, and have a solid understanding of fundraising. With excellent organizational skills, keen attention to detail, and efficiency, they engage professionally with donors, volunteers, and stakeholders, maintaining confidentiality and discretion with sensitive information. Their strong critical thinking skills enable effective problem\-solving, and they adeptly prioritize tasks while adapting to shifting responsibilities.

**Minimum Qualifications**

* Bachelor’s degree.
* **At least 3 years of fundraising experience, with a consistent record of securing gifts of $100,000\+.**
* Experience working within a major fundraising or capital campaign.
* Demonstrated ability to manage high‑level relationships and navigate complex interpersonal dynamics.
* Proven experience of managing and developing fundraising strategies for specific programs.

* Proven ability to navigate complex interpersonal dynamics with high emotional intelligence and professionalism.

The Mass General Brigham Development Office represents a team of 450\+ vibrant, collaborative, skilled fundraising professionals. We are dedicated to inspiring visionary philanthropy that advances caring and curing for patients in Boston and around the world. Philanthropy enables Mass General Brigham to deliver the highest quality patient care, pursue the most innovative and promising research, train the brightest minds to become the next generation of healthcare leaders and expand and improve our world\-class facilities. **Join us—Where your Career is a Force for Good!**
==================================================

**Job Description:**

Joining the American Red Cross is like nothing else – it’s as much something you feel as something you do. You become a vital part of the world’s largest humanitarian network. Joining a team of welcoming individuals who are exceptional yet unassuming. Diverse, yet uncompromising in unity. You grow your career within a movement that matters, where success is measured in people helped, communities made whole, and individuals equipped to never stop changing lives and situations for the better.


When you choose to be a force for good, you’ll have mentors who empower your growth along a purposeful career path. You align your life’s work with an ongoing mission that’s bigger than all of us. As you care for others, you’re cared for with competitive compensation and benefits. You join a community that respects who you are away from work as much as what you do while at work.


**Where Your Career is a Force for Good!**

The American Red Cross is seeking a **Chief Development Officer** to support the California Gold Country Region. This region supports 31 counties in Northern California. This is a hybrid position. Incumbents will spend several days a week in the office and the remainder of their time in the field or in a home office.


**WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW:**

In this role, you will lead a team of professional fundraisers to strengthen and grow the region’s donor base to meet its assigned financial target of more than $10M. The Chief Development Officer will provide leadership to the staff and volunteers in planning, managing, and implementing fundraising development strategies for the assigned segment(s) of giving. They will also assist in cultivating donors and sponsors to meet monthly, quarterly, and annual revenue targets while maintaining their own portfolios. The Director is responsible for managing an assigned team, including hiring, training, coaching, evaluating, and managing performance. This role is not eligible for relocation assistance.

* Develop and implement a plan for revenue growth from donors at the $5,000\+ level with an emphasis on maximizing revenue for the American Red Cross. The individual segment will collaborate with the Gift Planning team to strategically grow planned giving revenue across major donor\-managed accounts for individuals and families. The corporate and foundation segments’ strategic growth plan could include (but are not limited to) engagement and growth of giving societies such as the Tiffany Circle, serving as a staff liaison to the Board's Philanthropy Committee and developing and implementing strategies for board giving and influence for the region, growing cause marketing and overall corporate revenue by assisting corporations in achieving their goals to strengthen their brand, marketing, and employee engagement, leading the region's special events program, or leading a strategy to identify, cultivate, and engage foundations to grow their support for the American Red Cross.
* Cultivate relationships and serve as primary staff liaison for major donors and conduct regular joint visits with relationship managers and donors when those relationships are managed by members of your team.
* Collaborate with national headquarters development team members to maximize fundraising opportunities and implement policies and procedures to ensure the success of the region's fundraising efforts.
* Oversee the implementation of organizational campaigns in the region.
* Serve as a senior leader in the Region and proactively drive the implementation of strategic decisions for the organization. Ensure that the Region meets all reporting requirements related to fundraising, including ensuring up\-to\-date donor records in Salesforce.
* Provide strong leadership, develop, and set individual and team performance goals and manage/drive change.
* Hire, manage, train, and evaluate staff and volunteers to ensure fundraising results and that all team members meet or exceed national/regional fundraising metrics.
* Lead and execute disaster relief fundraising efforts as appropriate.

***WHAT YOU NEED TO SUCCEED:***

**Education:** Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience required.


**Experience:** Minimum 7 years of sales/fundraising experience or equivalent combination of education and related experience required. Experience with increasing philanthropic revenue from diverse constituencies, developing and implementing broad\-based fundraising programs, and knowledge of “best practices” in development.


**Management Experience:** 5 years of management experience.

Ability to work on a team within a collaborative environment and ensure the highest customer service orientation.

**About Redwood Community Services**
------------------------------------


Redwood Community Services (RCS) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to strengthening individuals, families, and communities through behavioral health, child welfare, youth development, housing, and support services. Our work changes lives every day, and our employees make that possible.


We are seeking an experienced, strategic, and collaborative Director of Lake County Programs to provide leadership and oversight for a diverse portfolio of behavioral health, child welfare, youth development, and community\-based services throughout Lake County. The ideal candidate is a mission\-driven leader with strong clinical knowledge, operational expertise, and a passion for supporting children, youth, families, and communities. This role requires the ability to balance program quality, fiscal stewardship, staff development, community partnerships, and innovative service delivery while ensuring excellence in care and outcomes.

**What You'll Do**


Key responsibilities include:


* Provide overall leadership, direction, and oversight for Lake County programs, ensuring high\-quality services across behavioral health, rehabilitation, substance use disorder treatment, foster care, wraparound, residential, and school\-based programs.
* Oversee clinical and program operations while promoting effective coordination of services for children, youth, families, and individuals with complex behavioral health and social service needs.
* Monitor program performance, revenue, and expenditures to ensure fiscal responsibility, sustainability, and alignment with organizational goals.
* Participate in strategic planning, program development, and implementation of new services that respond to emerging community needs and funding opportunities.
* Collaborate with clinical leadership to support treatment planning, program reviews, quality improvement activities, and resolution of reportable incidents and service concerns.
* Provide leadership in budget planning, review financial performance, and authorize expenditures consistent with approved budgets and organizational priorities.
* Recruit, interview, hire, supervise, mentor, and develop program managers, supervisors, clinicians, rehabilitation specialists, and support staff.
* Foster a positive, collaborative culture through staff development, training, coaching, performance management, and succession planning efforts.
* Work closely with leadership teams across RCS to ensure seamless coordination among programs and alignment with the agency's mission, values, and strategic objectives.
* Establish and maintain productive partnerships with county agencies, schools, child welfare organizations, behavioral health providers, regional centers, community partners, and other stakeholders.
* Represent RCS within community collaboratives, public meetings, and professional organizations to strengthen partnerships and advance services for vulnerable populations.
* Ensure compliance with licensing requirements, contractual obligations, organizational policies, and applicable regulatory standards while promoting continuous quality improvement.
* Prepare and review program reports, operational data, and outcome measures to support informed decision\-making and effective communication with executive leadership and the Board of Directors.
* Respond to complex operational issues, critical incidents, and crisis situations while maintaining a focus on safety, service quality, and positive outcomes.
* Support community engagement, public relations, fundraising, special projects, and organizational initiatives as assigned.

**What We're Looking For**


The successful candidate will bring:


* A master's degree in Behavioral Science, Social Work, Psychology, Counseling, or a related field.
* California BBS licensure (or eligibility consistent with program requirements).
* Significant leadership and supervisory experience in behavioral health, child welfare, youth services, or related community\-based programs.
* Experience managing complex programs, budgets, contracts, and multidisciplinary teams.
* Strong clinical judgment, strategic thinking, and problem\-solving skills.
* Excellent communication, relationship\-building, and community partnership abilities.
* A commitment to trauma\-informed, culturally responsive, family\-centered, and strengths\-based services.

**Why Join RCS?**


At RCS, you'll have the opportunity to lead meaningful programs that improve the lives of children, youth, and families while working alongside dedicated professionals who share a commitment to service, innovation, and community impact. You'll play a key role in shaping programs, developing staff, strengthening partnerships, and helping advance our mission of building healthier and stronger communities throughout Lake County.

**We offer:**

* Meaningful work with a respected nonprofit organization
* Collaborative and supportive team environment
* Opportunities for professional development
* Stable, mission\-focused employment
* Comprehensive benefits package (for eligible employees)
* The opportunity to make a real impact every day

**Apply Today****If you're passionate about accuracy, enjoy working behind the scenes to support employees, and want to be part of an organization that changes lives, we'd love to hear from you.**

**We're Searching for Our Next Chief Operating Officer**

Not simply to manage a law firm. **But to help continue building one of the most innovative family law firms in the country.**

Laughlin Legal, PC represents founders, venture capital professionals, technology executives, entrepreneurs, physicians, professional athletes, and other sophisticated clients throughout Silicon Valley and California. Our matters frequently involve complex financial issues, executive compensation, founder equity, private equity, closely held businesses, international issues, and high\-conflict custody litigation.

Over the past thirteen years, we have built one of California's fastest\-growing family law firms. Our gross revenue has increased by approximately 25% to 50% year over year for thirteen consecutive years.

Why? Because we are relentless in our pursuit of excellence. We strive to provide the highest level of legal representation and client service in family law in California. We invest in exceptional people, embrace innovation, continually refine our systems, and refuse to become complacent. We believe that when you build an extraordinary team and deliver an extraordinary client experience, sustained growth naturally follows.

Just as importantly, we believe extraordinary results should never come at the expense of extraordinary people.

We have intentionally built a collaborative, collegial culture where people genuinely enjoy working together, support one another, and are committed to each other's success. We value excellence, but we also value balance. Unlike many firms, our attorneys are expected to bill only **1,400 hours per year**, allowing them to spend meaningful time with their families, pursue personal interests, and build fulfilling lives outside the office.

We reject the notion that burnout is the price of success. Our experience has proven otherwise. By investing in exceptional people, creating outstanding systems, embracing innovation, and fostering a culture people are proud to be part of, we have achieved remarkable growth while maintaining a workplace where talented professionals choose to build long\-term careers.

**We're looking for an exceptional Chief Operating Officer who is energized by the opportunity to continue building one of the most innovative family law firms in the country.**

This is a true executive leadership position. You will serve as my strategic partner in shaping the future of the firm while leading our operations, people, technology, and long\-term growth. You will have a meaningful voice in the future direction of the firm and the opportunity to help build an organization that sets a new standard for family law.

Key Responsibilities

* Executive leadership and strategic planning
* Recruiting, retaining, and developing exceptional attorneys and professional staff
* Associate coaching, mentoring, leadership development, and performance management
* Elevating every aspect of the client experience
* Optimizing Clio and leveraging its full capabilities across the firm
* Designing scalable systems, workflows, and operational excellence
* Leading the implementation of artificial intelligence and automation throughout the firm
* Coordinating marketing and business development initiatives
* Financial management, budgeting, billing, collections, profitability, and key performance metrics
* Developing and implementing strategic growth and expansion initiatives
* Helping design the firm's future leadership and partnership structure

The Ideal Candidate Will Have

* Significant executive leadership experience within a successful law firm
* Demonstrated success building high\-performing teams and scalable operations
* Extensive experience with Clio (Clio Certified Consultant or Clio Partner experience is strongly preferred)
* Exceptional financial, operational, and business acumen
* Experience implementing technology and AI solutions to improve efficiency and client service
* Outstanding emotional intelligence, leadership, judgment, and communication skills
* An entrepreneurial mindset with an unwavering commitment to excellence
* A passion for building organizations, developing people, and creating a culture where others thrive

We are building more than a successful law firm.

We are building a place where exceptional professionals can do the best work of their careers without sacrificing the people and experiences that matter most. We believe a law firm can be both extraordinarily successful and deeply human. We believe innovation, kindness, accountability, and excellence can coexist. After thirteen years of sustained growth, we have demonstrated that they do.

If our next chapter excites you, we'd love to meet you.

Compensation

* **Base salary:** $280,000 \- $300,000, depending on experience
* Performance bonus
* Comprehensive benefits
* 3 weeks vacation
* 11 paid holidays

**Hybrid position. You must live in the SF Bay Area.**

Please send your résumé and a cover letter explaining why this opportunity speaks to you to **careers@laughlinlegal.com**.

**Great law firms are not built by great lawyers alone. They are built by visionary leaders who know how to attract exceptional people, create extraordinary systems, embrace innovation, and build organizations that endure.**

If you're excited by the opportunity to help build one of the premier family law firms in the nation, we'd love to hear from you!

Pay: $280,000\.00 \- $300,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

The County of Los Angeles Chief Executive Office is recruiting for an Executive Director, Racial Equity (UC). The Executive Director, Racial Equity will lead the Los Angeles County's enterprise racial equity strategy by providing executive leadership for the County’s Anti\-Racism, Diversity, and Inclusion (ARDI) initiative and serving as the County’s principal advisor on organizational transformation, equity strategy, implementation, and systems change. **JOB DESCRIPTION**  

  

  

Help shape the future of identity while protecting customers who trust us with sensitive information. In this role, you will make it simple and safe for customers to keep their identity data current and help catch and fix issues before they cause harm.


As an **executive director identity lifecycle** in **Trust and security**, you lead identity lifecycle management for customer demographic and identity updates, including phone, email, address, and legal name changes. You partner across channels, lines of business, and technology teams to deliver seamless customer experiences while putting strong protections in place during higher\-risk updates. You also own the strategy and roadmap for remediating potentially inaccurate or missing identity data and drive measurable improvements in data quality and account takeover prevention.


Job responsibilities

* Set the vision, strategy, and execution plan for how customers update identity and demographic data across channels.
* Partner with channels and lines of business to design and deliver simple, best\-in\-class update experiences.
* Build protections for high\-risk update moments, including possession and verification checks to help prevent account takeover.
* Create and deliver a roadmap to remediate potentially inaccurate or missing identity data, including proactive customer outreach and validation.
* Balance strong security with a smooth customer experience, reducing unnecessary friction for legitimate customers.
* Lead, mentor, and develop a team of product managers, building a culture of innovation, accountability, and excellence.
* Collaborate with technology, design, fraud, modeling, and operations teams to deliver scalable and reliable solutions.

Reporting to the Chief Mission Advancement Officer, the Director develops and executes the organization’s Annual Giving Campaign and additional year\-round fundraising strategies designed to increase donor acquisition, retention, engagement, and giving. The Coalition’s mission is to protect the biodiversity of the Sonoran Desert in Southern Arizona through science\-based advocacy, education, and collaboration. We are an alliance of conservation and community organizations that formed in February 1998\. Boys \& Girls Club of Central New Mexico


Salary: $70,000\-$80,000 Annually


FLSA Status: Exempt


Reports To: Chief Executive Officer / Chief Operating Officer


Location: Teen Center – Albuquerque, New Mexico


Position Summary


The Executive Director of the Teen Center is a visionary and results\-driven leader responsible for the overall management, strategic direction, growth, and success of the Boys \& Girls Club of Central New Mexico's Teen Center. This position provides leadership to staff, oversees program quality, develops sustainable systems and operational structures, ensures grant compliance, cultivates community partnerships, and creates a positive culture that empowers both youth and staff.


The Executive Director serves as the face of the Teen Center in the community and is responsible for building a high\-performing team dedicated to delivering exceptional youth development experiences. Through strong leadership, delegation, accountability, and collaboration, this position ensures that the Teen Center remains a safe, welcoming, innovative, and engaging environment where teens can achieve academic success, develop workforce readiness skills, strengthen mental wellness, and become leaders in their communities.


This position aligns with the Boys \& Girls Clubs of America (BGCA) Formula for Impact and Program Quality Standards, ensuring programs consistently promote Academic Success, Good Character \& Citizenship, and Healthy Lifestyles.


Essential Duties and Responsibilities


Strategic Leadership \& Vision


* Provide overall leadership and direction for Teen Center operations, programming, and strategic initiatives.
* Develop and implement long\-term goals that align with BGCCNM's mission, strategic plan, and community needs.
* Create and maintain systems that improve organizational efficiency, accountability, communication, and program effectiveness.
* Lead organizational change and continuous improvement efforts.
* Foster a culture of excellence, innovation, teamwork, and youth\-centered decision\-making.
* Ensure all Teen Center operations reflect BGCA's mission, values, and best practices.

Team Development \& Organizational Culture


* Recruit, supervise, coach, and develop a diverse team of skilled professionals.
* Cultivate a positive workplace culture focused on collaboration, accountability, professional growth, and high performance.
* Provide regular supervision, performance evaluations, and professional development opportunities.
* Empower Program Directors and leadership staff through effective delegation and shared ownership of organizational goals.
* Facilitate leadership team meetings and ensure alignment across all program areas.
* Promote clear communication and collaboration among departments.

Program Oversight \& Quality Assurance


* Oversee the development, implementation, and evaluation of all Teen Center programs.
* Ensure programs meet BGCA Program Quality Standards and support Academic Success, Workforce Readiness, Mental Wellness, Character Development, and Leadership Development.
* Collaborate with Program Directors to establish measurable outcomes and program goals.
* Monitor participation, engagement, and retention data to drive continuous improvement.
* Ensure youth voice and feedback are incorporated into program planning and decision\-making.
* Create an engaging, inclusive, and welcoming environment that encourages teen participation and belonging.

Grant Management \& Compliance


* Collaborate with Program Directors and administrative staff to ensure compliance with all grant requirements.
* Oversee collection and reporting of program data, attendance, outcomes, and performance metrics.
* Ensure timely submission of reports for federal, state, local, and private grants.
* Work closely with leadership to identify funding opportunities and support grant development efforts.
* Monitor grant deliverables, budgets, and performance measures.
* Maintain compliance with all funding agency requirements, including 21st Century Community Learning Centers and other youth development funding sources.

Community Relations \& Partnership Development


* Serve as the primary ambassador and spokesperson for the Teen Center.
* Represent Boys \& Girls Club of Central New Mexico at community meetings, public events, schools, networking opportunities, and stakeholder gatherings.
* Develop and maintain strategic partnerships with schools, businesses, government agencies, community organizations, higher education institutions, and mental health providers.

* Oversee budgeting and resource allocation in collaboration with organizational leadership.
* Monitor expenditures and ensure responsible stewardship of financial resources.

Youth Engagement \& Teen Center Culture


* Foster a Teen Center culture that is safe, inclusive, innovative, and youth\-centered.
* Ensure youth have opportunities to participate in leadership development, workforce readiness, mental health support, academic assistance, and community engagement.
* Promote meaningful relationships between youth and staff.
* Support special events, community service projects, leadership conferences, and youth recognition opportunities.
* Ensure the Teen Center remains responsive to the evolving needs of young people and families.

Education


Bachelor's Degree required in:


* Nonprofit Management
* Education
* Social Work
* Public Administration
* Business

The Executive Director will serve as the founding scientific and strategic leader of the AI Research and Innovation Institute, guiding its growth into a globally recognized center of excellence in artificial intelligence. The Full Professor position will be housed in GVSU’s College of Computing, with an adjunct appointment at the Van Andel Institute. new_jobs.title: Senior Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
**About Journey Found:**  

Headquartered in Manchester, CT, and established in 2013, Journey Found (JF) is a proven leader in providing behavioral, residential, and many other support services to Connecticut individuals with Pervasive Developmental Disorders and Intellectual Disabilities, enabling them to become all they are capable of being. Journey Found currently serves 73 adults with intellectual disabilities ranging in age from 21\-82 in small (mostly 3\-person) group homes and apartments, in individualized day support services, and a growing number in their own or their family’s homes. Services are person\-centered, and each person has an individual plan of service that addresses their hopes, wants, and needs in each aspect of their life: home, work, recreation, medical, and sometimes education.  

Journey Found’s annual budget is $19 million, 95% of which comes from contracts with the state of Connecticut. The organization has 292 employees (\~166 FTEs) and is governed by an actively engaged 10\-member board.

 **Opportunity Going Forward:**  

The Senior Director of Operations provides oversight of all the agency's operations and acts as the internal face of the organization. This role is critical in helping fulfill Journey Found’s mission of providing the best person\-centered services to people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and to secure its sustainability in service to this mission.  

The Senior Director of Operations is a dynamic leader and creative individual who works to maintain and expand the high\-quality services provided by the organization. The candidate is willing to learn and remains flexible enough to adjust priorities to ensure the organization continues to fulfill its mission as new service trends emerge, and changes in the environment occur. Journey Found’s next Senior Director of Operations will join the team at an exciting time. The field is changing, with innovations in technology presenting new ways to serve the people Journey Found supports. The new Senior Director of Operations will have the opportunity to work with the CEO to explore and realize the impact of new service models that align with Journey Found’s person\-centered culture, leaving an imprint on the organization and the lives it touches. In addition, there is a strong team in place, and the new Senior Director of Operations will be able to partner with team leaders who have been providing high\-quality services to the people JF serves while looking for growth opportunities within the organization.  

The Senior Director of Operations reports directly to the Chief Executive Officer under the general direction of the Board of Directors. The position is based in JF’s Manchester, CT office and is expected to be on\-site for the majority of the Monday\-Friday work week, with the flexibility to work remotely as their schedule and commitments permit. This individual has direct supervisory responsibilities for five professionals in the role of Director of Support Services, the Facilities Manager, and the Director of Clinical Services. This role indirectly supervises the organization’s nursing department and approximately 250 Direct Support Professionals.




**The Senior Director of Operations Key Responsibilities Include:**

 **Management and Administration**  

* Foster a work culture of success, empowerment, team building, and reward organizational effectiveness.
* Effectively resolve conflicts, deal with challenging situations, and make smart decisions.
* Assure compliance with federal, state, and local regulations, statutes, licensure, accreditation (where applicable), and agency policy.
* Provide on\-call support to the organization.
* Critically evaluate situations and strategically plan solutions.
* Firmly, promptly, and effectively manage crisis situations.
* Adeptly collect and analyze data to inform decisions that lead to positive outcomes.
**Organizational Engagement**  

* Make strong connections with the staff and people supported.
* Strongly focus on stakeholder engagement.
* Effectively communicate with families of people supported.
* Approach working with teams and mediating positive outcomes in a proactive manner.
**Clinical Oversight**  

* Partner with the Director of Clinical Services to ensure high\-quality services are provided.
* Ensure the Clinical Team completes continuing education to promote learning and best practice approaches to clinical supports.
* Effectuate a Person\-Centered approach using applied behavior analysis (ABA) techniques and strategies.
**Facilities Oversight**  

* Develop and evaluate proactive facilities management plans.
* Ensure all Journey Found homes are maintained to the highest standards, and they reflect positively upon their communities.
* Manage a network of vendors and property managers in support of Journey Found’s mission.
* Negotiate contractual agreements and manage capital improvement projects.
* Maximize the efforts of the Facilities department by triaging work orders.



**Desired Credentials/Profile of the Ideal Candidate:**  

There are innumerable ways to learn, grow, and excel professionally with Journey Found. We respect this when we review applications and take a broad look at the experience of each applicant. We want to get to know you and the unique strengths you will bring to the work. We will most likely be interested in your candidacy if you demonstrate most of the qualifications and attributes listed below:  

* A sincere commitment to Journey Found’s mission of delivering high\-quality, person\-centered services to people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.
* Five or more years of managerial and administrative responsibilities in a non\-profit organization or an equivalent combination of training and experience.
* At least three years of experience working with the CT Department of Developmental Services (DDS).
* An established track record of fostering a successful and effective team\-base

new_jobs.description:
**Job Description:**

**POSITION SUMMARY**


The Chief Operating Officer (COO) is a senior executive who provides strategic leadership and oversees the integration and growth of Veit’s construction operations in line with the company’s 2032 Vision to become the Midwest’s most trusted infrastructure solutions provider. This role develops and implements forward\-thinking strategies to ensure safe, reliable project delivery, exceptional client experiences, and innovation. The COO builds scalable teams, enhances leadership and technical skills, and adapts operations to market and client needs.


With a strong background in Heavy Civil construction, the COO turns enterprise strategy into actionable plans, leading operations across all service lines and markets. The COO drives collaboration, breaks down organizational silos, and champions multidivisional initiatives and joint projects.


Additionally, the COO supports team versatility through cross\-training, rotational assignments, and robust operational training. By continuously improving standard operating procedures and sharing best practices, the COO enables responsible growth, market differentiation, and long\-term value for clients and communities.

* Provide overall leadership and integration of construction operations across all service lines and markets.
* Ensure consistent execution of operational standards, project delivery models, and performance expectations.

* Own and enforce enterprise go/no\-go criteria aligned to Veit’s strategic focus, margin thresholds, and resource capacity.
* Ensure operational leaders consistently prioritize negotiated, multi\-service, union\-friendly projects aligned with Veit’s core markets and strategic clients.
* Champion the elimination of organizational silos by encouraging and facilitating multidivisional initiatives and joint project delivery.

* Champion intentional development of technical, leadership, and business acumen skills at all levels of operations.
